entries.title: Abhaysinha Gunvantrao Shelake
entries.excerpt: Dr. Abhaysinha Gunvantrao Shelake is a dedicated Assistant Professor in the Department of Civil Engineering at Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University (MITWPU), Pune. With over ten years of teaching experience, he specializes in Construction Engineering and Management. He has completed his B.E. in Civil Engineering and M.E. in Construction and Management from Savitribai Phule Pune University. he earned his Ph.D. in Civil Engineering from Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University (MITWPU), Pune, titled "Development of a comprehensive framework in reducing delays for predictable risks." Dr. Shelake is actively engaged in teaching both undergraduate and postgraduate students. His teaching portfolio includes key subjects like Project Management, Project Scheduling & Material Management, Earned Value Management, Project Risk Management, and Surveying & Geomatics at UG and PG level. His pedagogical approach combines practical knowledge with software applications, making use of advanced tools such as Autodesk AutoCAD, Civil 3D, REVIT, NAVISWORK, Microsoft Project, and Primavera. He is certified by Autodesk and holds credentials including a Six Sigma Green Belt, ISO 9001:2015 QMS Internal Auditor, and a Project Management Professional Plus (PMP+) certification from Simplilearn Academy. Throughout his academic career, he has guided over 24 postgraduate and undergraduate research projects. He has significantly contributed to curriculum development and academic coordination at MITWPU, holding roles such as Academic Coordinator and Website Coordinator. He is a core member of the Board of Studies Sub-Committee and the PMQIC Committee. He has also coordinated numerous national-level immersion programs and industrial visits to premier infrastructure projects, offering students valuable real-world exposure. His research primarily focuses on project risk management, scheduling delays, and the integration of advanced decision-making tools in construction management. His Ph.D. work has led to the development of innovative frameworks to improve delay prediction and mitigation in tunnel construction projects named as IRPAD (Integrated Risk Prioritization for Determination of Activity-wise Delay". He is also actively involved in exploring sustainable construction materials, the application of augmented and virtual reality, and the adoption of Building Information Modeling (BIM) technologies. He has published 18+ research papers in reputed international journals, including SCOPUS- and SCIE- indexed publications, and presented several papers in national and international conferences. Additionally, he has authored eight textbooks tailored for Civil Engineering programs under Savitribai Phule Pune University and Mumbai University. His authored books span topics such as Construction Management, Construction Safety, Smart Building Materials, and Airport & Bridge Engineering. A life member of the Indian Society for Technical Education (ISTE), Dr. Shelake is committed to academic excellence, research innovation, and mentoring students toward industry-readiness.

entries.title: Abhijit Manikrao Bhagat
entries.excerpt: Abhijit Bhagat brings over two decades of industry experience, along with more than ten years of shaping young designers in the classroom. He understands the realities of the field, having worked on major projects with Mahindra & Mahindra, Bajaj Auto, and Tata Motors, before going on to establish his own architecture practice.

entries.title: Abhishek Prakash Chunawale
entries.department: Department of Computer Engineering and Technology
entries.excerpt: Dr. Abhishek Prakash Chunawale is an Assistant Professor at the School of Computer Science and Engineering at Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University in Pune, India. With 9.5 years of teaching experience, he received his B.E. in IT from Sant Gadge Baba Amravati University in 2011, where he was a Gold Medalist, and his M.E. in IT from Savitribai Phule Pune University in 2014. He has completed his PhD in Computer Engineering, and his research area is Affective Computing. He has research paper publications in prestigious international journals and conferences such as IEEE and Springer. His areas of interest include IoT, Information and Cyber Security, Social Media Privacy and Security, and AR/VR.

entries.title: Adinath Dnyandev Yadhav
entries.excerpt: Rigidity Analysis of structures, Optimization, Offshore Structures
entries.text: Adinath Dnyandev Yadhav Assistant Professor Department of Civil Engineering Rigidity Analysis of structures, Optimization, Offshore Structures Prof. Adinath Yadhav, an Assistant Professor at MIT World Peace University, Pune. With 9 years of teaching experience under his belt, he completed his B.E. in Civil Engineering from Savitribai Phule Pune University and his MTech in Marine Structures from National Institute of Technology, Surathkal. He has completed Ph.D. in Structural Engineering at MITWPU. Design of RCC Structures, Design of Steel Structures, Theory of Structures, Subsea Engineering, Strength of Materials, Analysis of Structures, Engineering Mechanics, Advanced Design of Steel Structures, Plastic Analysis of Steel Structures. 1. Yadhav, A., Gosavi, S. & Kulkarni, M. Nonlinear behaviour of a reinforced concrete building subjected to blast load and optimisation using a meta-heuristic algorithm. Asian J Civ Eng 25, 397–412 (2024). https://doi.org/10.1007/s42107-023-00783-2 2. Yadhav, A., Kulkarni, M. Optimal design of FPSO topside module for in-place, lift and weighing conditions utilizing meta- heuristic optimization algorithms. Asian J Civ Eng 24, 3055–3070 (2023). https://doi.org/10.1007/s42107-023-00694-2 3. Daivik P., Yadhav A. D. (2021). Comparative Analysis of Deep Beams using Steel and Carbon rods as reinforcement.

entries.title: Akashdeep Sharma
entries.excerpt: Architecture, Spiritual Architecture, Concrete
entries.text: Akashdeep Sharma Assistant Professor Department of Design Architecture, Spiritual Architecture, Concrete Akashdeep Sharma is an Assistant Professor with over Eight years of academic and professional experience in the fields of architecture and interior design. He holds a Master of Architecture (M.Arch) in Construction Management and is currently pursuing a Ph.D. in Design and Architecture at Symbiosis International University (SIU). He is a Certified Architect registered with the Council of Architecture, New Delhi. Interior Design Studio I, Interior Design Studio II, Kiosk Design.

entries.title: Akshat Bhargava
entries.excerpt: Akshat Bhargava is currently an Assistant Professor in the School of Business at Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University, Pune, India and a Doctoral Candidate at the Indian Institute of Management, Shillong, India. He holds a Bachelor of Engineering (B.E) in Information Technology (IT) degree from MJP Rohilkhand University, Bareilly and a Master of Human Resource Management (MHRM) degree from Indian Institute of Social Welfare & Business Management (IISWBM), University of Calcutta. His research interests cover talent management, leadership development in social enterprises, social stock exchanges, impact investment, performance of social enterprises, business analytics, organizational network analysis and public policy frameworks for nature positive economies. His dissertation work has been published in the journals of international repute. He has also presented papers in several International Conferences. In addition to his doctoral work, Akshat has worked for around 15 years in different private and public sector organizations.

entries.title: Arun Balaso Mali
entries.excerpt: Mechanical Design, Compliant Mechanisms Condition Monitoring
entries.text: Arun Balaso Mali Assistant Professor Department of Mechanical Engineering Mechanical Design, Compliant Mechanisms Condition Monitoring Mr. Arun Mali is currently working as Assistant Professor in Mechanical Engineering Department of Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University, Kothrud Pune. He is having more than 12 years of teaching experiance. He holds Masters of Engineering in Design Engineering Domain and Currently pursuing Ph. D. in Mechanical Engineering. Master's work was associated with Design and Numerical Experiment on Compliant Mechanisms. Previously worked on Flexure mechanisms in Precision Applications. Research work was coined to achieve a specific objective of demonstration of dual use of Voice Coil Motor (i.e as actuator as well as sensor) which eliminates need of sensor in precision scanning applications . It was planned to design frictionless, backlash free XY flexural mechanism and experimentally demonstrate the dual use of VCM. His research areas are Compliant Mechanisms, Mechanical Design, Vibrations and acoustics, condition monitoring of Machines etc. A devoted educator, he focuses on inspiring students to engage critically with Engineering and Technology while contributing meaningfully to the study of diverse perspectives. His commitment to fostering intellectual growth reflects his belief in the transformative power of education.

entries.title: Ashish Singh Tomar
entries.excerpt: Ashish believes that design education should be hands-on, empathetic, and rooted in real human needs. His goal is not just to teach tools and techniques, but to help students observe deeply, think critically, and build solutions that truly matter. What drives him is the joy of seeing young minds grow curious and confident. At MIT-WPU, he hopes to create a warm, collaborative learning space where students feel encouraged to experiment, fail, and learn. With his background in product development and UX, he would love to contribute to projects that blend technology with social impact, staying true to the institution's vision of nurturing responsible, innovative leaders.

Currently serving as an Assistant Professor at MIT WPU University , she has a distinguished track record in teaching, curriculum development, and research, particularly in the areas of ERP implementation and commerce. Dr. Rathore holds a Ph.D. in Commerce, an MBA in Finance, M. Com and is a Chartered Accountant, bringing a rich blend of academic knowledge and practical expertise to her role. She has made significant contributions, authoring two books, numerous scholarly papers, securing two patents, and obtaining one copyright for innovative teaching methodologies. Her career is marked by innovative leadership, successful program coordination, and a commitment to fostering student growth and success.

entries.text: Dr. Aachal Taywade Assistant Professor Department of Liberal Arts Personality, Smartphone Addiction, Parenting, youth mental health, and interpersonal relationships. Dr. Aachal is a distinguished academician and researcher, holding a PhD in Psychology with a specialization in Personality Factors, Psychopathology, Intelligence, and Smartphone Usage Behavior of Adolescents. A gold medalist in her MA Psychology program, she has in depth understanding of areas like Developmental Psychology, Educational Psychology, Personality Psychology, and Smartphone Addiction.

entries.title: Dr. Aaditi Khare
entries.excerpt: Mrs. Aaditi A Khare is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Liberal Arts having a Teaching experience of 10 years in the field of Psychology at undergraduate and postgraduate level. Her teaching philosophy focuses on clear understanding, practical learning, and active student participation. She is motivated by the desire to help students develop strong subject knowledge, critical thinking, and good values. She believes in creating a supportive and engaging classroom environment that encourages curiosity and continuous learning. In line with MIT-WPU’s vision, She aims to contribute through effective teaching, innovative methods, and academic commitment, helping students grow into skilled professionals and responsible individuals who can positively contribute to society. Her PhD is in the domain of undergraduate college students’ well-being.

entries.excerpt: Natural Products

entries.title: Dr. Abhijit Bendre
entries.excerpt: Cosmic magnetic fields, nonlinear and galactic dynamos, the interstellar medium, and cosmic-ray dynamos.
entries.text: Dr. Abhijit Bendre Assistant Professor Department of Physics Cosmic magnetic fields, nonlinear and galactic dynamos, the interstellar medium, and cosmic-ray dynamos. Abhijit B. Bendre earned his PhD in Astrophysics from the Leibniz Institute for Astrophysics in Potsdam. He has been a researcher at several institutions, including the Inter-University Centre for Astronomy and Astrophysics (IUCAA) in Pune, India, the Ecole Polytechnique Federale de Lausanne (EPFL) in Switzerland and Scuola Normale Superiore (SNS) in Pisa in Italy. His research interests are cosmic magnetic fields, nonlinear dynamo theory, galactic dynamos, the interstellar medium (ISM), and cosmic ray dynamos. His past research focuses on exploring the mechanisms behind the generation of magnetic fields in various celestial objects and their connection to turbulence. entries.title: Dr. Abhijith B
entries.excerpt: Abhijith B serves as an Assistant Professor at the Dadasaheb Phalke International Film Institute, MIT-WPU. He holds a PhD in Cultural Studies from The English and Foreign Languages University, Hyderabad. His study concentrates on contemporary Malayalam cinema, particularly addressing issues of form, space, and subjectivity. By prioritising the ideology of form as a primary theoretical framework, his work analyses the historical transformations influencing Indian cinema in a broader context. His study examines the intertwining of geography and story as a crucial indicator of cinema's social component in the contemporary era, especially in the absence of concrete social markers under the neoliberal context. His study has been published in peer-reviewed journals, and he is presently examining the correlation between contemporary genre forms and the issue of political federalism in Indian cinema.

entries.title: Dr. Ajay Sahu
entries.excerpt: Dr. Ajay K. Sahu has completed his doctoral study (Ph.D.) from IIT Kharagpur with special focus on “Characterization of fracture networks from outcrop analogous: A flow simulation approach”. During his doctoral study, He has also carried out collaborative research work in the area of reservoir modelling and fluid flow simulation. He has published research papers in several reputed journals and also presented his works at several national and international conferences. He pursued his post-graduation (M. Tech) in Petroleum Engineering from IIT(ISM) Dhanbad and completed his graduation (B. Tech) in Petroleum Engineering from Dibrugarh University, Dibrugarh. His research area of interests includes Fractured Reservoir Simulation and Characterization, Flow in Porous Media, Optimized Filed Development Approaches, Enhanced Oil Recovery: Screening and Application, Matlab in Geoscience, Python for Oil and Gas. Presently, he is teaching basic and applied courses at undergraduate and post graduate. He also mentors students for their research projects. He is also an active professional member of oil and gas scientific societies like; SPE, SEG, ARMA, AAPG and EGU.

entries.title: Dr. Ajit Baburao Deore
entries.excerpt: Corrosion science, including anti-corrosive films on steel.
entries.text: Dr. Ajit Baburao Deore Assistant Professor Department of Physics Corrosion science, including anti-corrosive films on steel. Dr. Ajit B Deore is an Assistant Professor of Physics with expertise in Corrosion Techniques, particularly anti-corrosive films on steel. He completed his post-graduation in Physics with a specialization in Electronics. Dr. Deore's research interests focus on the development and application of various corrosion techniques to prevent or mitigate the damaging effects of corrosion on different materials. His research has a specific emphasis on developing anti-corrosive films on steel to increase the material's durability and lifespan. Dr. Deore is committed to advancing the field of corrosion techniques and has contributed significantly to this area through his research and publications.

entries.excerpt: She is a faculty member specializing in Operations and Supply Chain Management. Her areas of interest include process optimization, logistics management, and strategic supply chain design, with a focus on connecting theoretical knowledge to practical business challenges.

She graduated in Electronics Engineering from Dr. Babasaheb Ambedkar Marathwada University, Aurangabad in 1978, and completed her post-graduation in Electronics-VLSI Design from Bharati Vidyapeeth Deemed University, Pune, India in 2006. Phil.) and master’s degree in the Economics Area. Fundamentally, he believes in a pedagogy where the knowledge cultivated at institutions has to be nurtured in real-world settings. Teaching provides him the opportunity to practice what he has researched, yet at the same time, it helps the students stay updated with the recent advancements in the field of Economics. Thus, it emerges as a win-win situation for all the institute's stakeholders. Importantly, Dr. Yadav would like to develop an innovative teaching approach which may promote progressive critical thinking and create inclusive learning experiences that align with the institution’s academic values. He has published various research papers in highly reputed journals.

entries.title: Dr. Amruta Sameer Naik
entries.excerpt: Stem cell and bone biology, regenerative medicine, tissue engineering, biomaterial scaffolds, and osteomicrobiology.
entries.text: Dr. Amruta Sameer Naik Assistant Professor Department of Biosciences and Technology Stem cell and bone biology, regenerative medicine, tissue engineering, biomaterial scaffolds, and osteomicrobiology. Dr. Amruta Naik is working as an Assistant Professor at the Department of Biosciences and Technology, MIT-WPU, Pune. She is a goal-driven researcher and academician with a research experience of around 12 years in the field of Stem Cell Biology and Bone Biology. Dr. Amruta has received Ph.D. in Biotechnology from the University of Pune. Her research interests mainly include invention of new strategies for the application of Mesenchymal Stem Cells for the treatment of bone degenerative disorders to get better therapeutic effects. Dr. Amruta has received two research grants from DBT and DST, Govt. of India for perusing research in the field of stem cell biology and regenerative medicine. During her post-doctoral research work, she was also involved in collaborative research projects that studied the in vitro biocompatibility and osteointegration of scaffolds for bone and cartilage regeneration. She has published 14 research and review articles in peer-reviewed journals of international repute. Currently Dr. Amruta Naik is serving as a Guest Editor for Cancer Reports (Wiley) and JoVE. entries.title: Dr. Anik Karan
entries.excerpt: Dr. Anik Karan, Ph.D., MRSC is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Bioengineering at Dr. Vishwanath D. Karad MIT-World Peace University, Pune. His research spans across multiple fields, including biomaterials science, neuroscience, cancer biology, tissue engineering and biomedical imaging. Dr. Karan previously worked as a Postdoctoral Research Associate at the Department of Surgery and Transplant at the University of Nebraska Medical Center, Omaha, Nebraska, USA and the Department of Radiation Oncology and Department of Bioengineering at the University of Kansas Medical School and University of Kansas, contributing to studies on tissue engineering related to chronic wound healing and bone regeneration. He also has more than two years of experience in the biotechnology industry under L’Oreal, USA and Cl Lab LLC, Maryland, USA. In L’Oreal, he has worked on developing sustainable natural hair dye delivery systems through clay-based nano-carriers and in Cl Lab LLC., generating defined disease models for preclinical studies in the areas of type 2 diabetes treatment and Heart Failure with Preserved Ejection Fraction (HFpEF) treatments. He has done his Master of Science and Doctor of Philosophy in Biomedical Engineering from Louisiana Tech University, Louisiana, USA, on the topic of the development of novel biodegradable metal-organic conjugates and their applications in the areas of wound healing and cancer treatments. He has published extensively in leading Q1 journals, including Acta Biomaterialia, Materials Today Bio, Aging Research Reviews, Advanced Science and ACS Applied Nanomaterials. He serves as a review editor for several reputed Q1 journals from Elsevier, ACS, RSC, Taylor & Francis, Springer Nature, BMC, Frontiers and MDPI, including RSC Advances, RSC New Journal of Chemistry, BMC Cancer, ACS Applied Biomaterials Science and Engineering, and Springer Nature Advanced Composites and Hybrid Materials. He is a full member of the American Chemical Society (ACS), USA, Royal Society of Chemistry (RSC), UK, Sigma Xi, USA and the Biomedical Engineering Society (BMES), USA. His research focuses on advanced therapeutic treatments for chronic wounds and bone defects using various combinatorial therapeutic modalities involving various synergistic biomaterials with various non-invasive treatment methods, such as electrical impulses and focused ultrasound.

entries.title: Dr. Anita Kumari
entries.excerpt: Behavioural Finance, Financial Management
entries.text: Dr. Anita Kumari Assistant Professor Department of Business Behavioural Finance, Financial Management Dr. Anita Kumari is a dedicated academician and finance professional with a strong background in both research and teaching. She holds a PhD in Finance, an MBA in Finance, with extensive academic experience in the field of financial management and analysis. With deep expertise in statistical analysis tools such as SPSS, SEM, PLS, SEM, and AMOS. Her academic interests span financial markets, investment analysis, and corporate finance. She is actively engaged in research and has contributed to various national and international journals. Dr. Kumari is committed to developing industry- relevant financial acumen among her students and promoting ethical financial practices.

entries.title: Dr. Ankita Agarwal
entries.excerpt: Dr. Ankita Agarwal is an experienced academician and researcher who holds a PhD in Computational Biology from IIT Kharagpur and Gold medal in M. Tech. (Bioinformatics) from West Bengal University of Technology. With a background in Chemistry and Bioinformatics, she has transitioned into the field of Bioengineering and Chemical Engineering, and joined as faculty at MIT-WPU. She brings a unique blend of expertise in both experimental and computational techniques, and has published research articles in leading scientific journals. Dr. Agarwal teaches a professional elective course of Bioinformatics and regular courses such as Data Science for engineers, Python for Bioinformatics and Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning in Biology to undergraduate students. With more than six year of previous research experience in DBT and CSIR-funded projects on computational analysis and prediction of protein-RNA interactions in human RNA-binding proteins, Dr. Agarwal’s current interest of research is to understand the sequence-structure-function paradigm of protein-RNA complexes especially in human RNA-binding proteins. With demonstrated expertise in computational biology, Dr. Agarwal is committed to fostering innovative approaches in teaching and research, and mentoring students towards achieving their full potential.

entries.title: Dr. Apurv Chaitanya Nellikka
entries.excerpt: Experimental nonlinear and quantum optics, structured light, and nanophotonics.
entries.text: Dr. Apurv Chaitanya Nellikka Assistant Professor Department of Physics Experimental nonlinear and quantum optics, structured light, and nanophotonics. Dr. Apurv Chaitanya Nellikka received his Integrated M.Sc. in Physics from Amrita University in 2012. He qualified for several entrance tests, including JAM, GATE, JEST, PRL (Ahmedabad), IPR (Gandhinagar), and IISc (Bangalore), before joining the Physical Research Laboratory, Ahmedabad, as a Ph.D. scholar in 2012. He earned his Ph.D. in 2016 from the Indian Institute of Technology, Gandhinagar, India. After graduation, he was a postdoctoral investigator at Tecnologico de Monterrey, Mexico, and gained research experience as a guest scientist at the Max Planck Institute for the Science of Light (Germany), Max Planck – uOttawa Centre for Extreme Quantum Photonics (Canada), and The Institute of Photonic Sciences (Spain). Before taking up his current position at MIT- WPU, he was a research professor at Tecnologico de Monterrey, Puebla, Mexico.

entries.title: Dr. Aquil Ahmad Khan
entries.excerpt: Dr. Aquil Ahmad Khan believes that education in media and communication should effectively bridge theory, practice, and critical inquiry to foster students' intellectual and professional growth. He strives to create an interactive, research-driven learning environment that connects academic concepts with real-world media practices. His teaching approach combines conceptual clarity with experiential learning, enabling students to engage in newsroom simulations, fieldwork, and collaborative projects through courses such as Television Journalism, Digital Journalism, and Media Production. Drawing on his industry experience, he enriches classroom discussions with practical insights and contemporary perspectives. Aligned with MIT World Peace University's philosophy of life transformation, Dr. Khan emphasizes ethical responsibility, social awareness, and value-based education, nurturing skilled, reflective, and socially responsible media professionals.

Dr. Ashish Andhale is an Assistant Professor at the School of Economics and Commerce, Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT-World Peace University. With a Ph.D. in Economics from Gokhale Institute of Politics and Economics, his research dives into the spatial dynamics of India's automobile component industry. His research, published in prestigious journals, explores themes from regional economics to international trade, reflecting his deep interest in the location of economic activity and spatial economics. His journey is a testament to his commitment to advancing economic thought and fostering a new generation of thinkers.

entries.title: Dr. Ashish Haribhau Pawar
entries.excerpt: Prof. Ashish Pawar is an Assistant Professor in the School of Mechanical Engineering at Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University, Pune. He has 9 years of experience in teaching and research, as well as 1 year of industry experience. Prof. Pawar is currently pursuing his PhD in "Experimental study on Hybrid ECM for machining of High Temperature Titanium alloys" at Savitribai Phule Pune University. He has published more than 10 papers in the field of Robotics, Non-Conventional Machining, and Design and Optimization. Prof. Pawar has also been involved in organizing workshops and conferences, such as SMF2022. He is in charge of admissions-related activities for the School of Mechanical Engineering and is currently guiding the Electric Two Wheeler Team of MITWPU, which won the overall 1st Rank in a competition organized by IIT GOA and ATHER. entries.title: Dr. Asim Samanta
entries.excerpt: Dr. Asim Samanta is currently serving as an Assistant Professor in the School of Health Sciences and Technology at Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University, Pune, India. He holds an M. Pharm (Pharmaceutics) degree from Jadavpur University, Kolkata, India, and a PhD from the Department of Pharmacy at the National University of Singapore (NUS). He qualified GATE 2005 with an All India Rank of 46. Dr. Samanta has over two years of postdoctoral research experience at NUS and the University of the Pacific, California, USA, along with nine years of extensive industrial experience at Johnson & Johnson and Encube Ethicals. His expertise lies in Novel Drug Delivery System, Process Analytical Technology (PAT), NIR and Raman spectroscopy, chemometrics, multivariate data analysis, and Quality by Design (QbD). He has published widely in reputed international journals and has presented his research at numerous national and international conferences.

entries.title: Dr. Atanu Hazarika
entries.excerpt: Dr. Atanu Hazarika is an Assistant Professor at the School of Business, MIT World Peace University in Pune, India. He is a Post-Doctoral Fellow from the Indian Institute of Management Bangalore. The title of the Post Doctoral research is ‘Developing sustainability-related knowledge and capability for farmers, Case Study of an Irrigation Tank project’. The project is sponsored by the Hanns Seidel Stiftung foundation (HSS), Germany. His PhD has been awarded by the Indian Institute of Technology, Kharagpur. His doctoral research work has focused on the issues of productivity and efficiency of paddy farming in Assam. His attempts to develop an interdependence (multivariate) framework for measuring the adoption rate of multiple modern technology practices. Additionally, the study attempts to find the technical efficiency and allocative efficiency level of the farmers. In addition, he worked on several projects sponsored by national and international agencies like NOVOD, SANDEE, ICSSR, and NABARD during his PhD tenure. The projects experienced give an exposure to work in different domains like the adoption and expansion behaviour of the Jatropha farmers, the economic valuation of biodiesel, the evaluation of watershed programs, and the financial risk attitude of the handloom micro-entrepreneur. He has worked as a Guest Faculty at Tezpur University, Assam. His teaching interests are Micro-economics, Macro-economics, Econometrics, Statistics, Development Economics, Operations Research, and Mathematical Economics. His research interests lie in the areas of Economics of Decision Choice, Efficiency and Productivity Analyses, Biofuel, Supply Chain, and Economic Valuation.

His doctoral research work has focused on the issues of productivity and efficiency of paddy farming in Assam. His attempts to develop an interdependence (multivariate) framework for measuring the adoption rate of multiple modern technology practices. Additionally, the study attempts to find the technical efficiency and allocative efficiency level of the farmers. In addition, he worked on several projects sponsored by national and international agencies like NOVOD, SANDEE, ICSSR, and NABARD during his PhD tenure. The projects experienced give an exposure to work in different domains like the adoption and expansion behaviour of the Jatropha farmers, the economic valuation of biodiesel, the evaluation of watershed programs, and the financial risk attitude of the handloom micro-entrepreneur. entries.title: Dr. Bishnu Prasad Mohapatra
entries.excerpt: Dr. Bishnu Prasad Mohapatra is presently working as Assistant Professor in School of Liberal Arts, Dr. Viswanath Karad MIT-World Peace University, Pune and involved in Teaching and Research in Political Science. Dr. Mohapatra has about 25 years of experience in Teaching and Research in Political Science and peace studies. Specialised in governance, public policy, decentralisation, local democracy and economic development. He has obtained M.Phil in Political Science from Berhampur University, Odisha, India and completed his PhD from Centre for Economic and Social Studies (CESS) Hyderabad in Political Science in 2018. Dr. Mohapatra has been extensively worked in Research Studies in various parts in India. He has published 28 numbers of Journal Articles in various reputed journals apart from 2 Books and 9 Chapters in Edited Books. He has also published 3 Research Monographs and associated with many global professional bodies, policy think-tanks and Journals as Mentor, Editorial Board Member, Advisor and Expert Members.

entries.text: Dr. Jyotsna Srivastava Assistant Professor Department of Peace Studies Peace and Conflict Studies, Migration Studies, Gender Studies, Masculinity, mobility and aspirations, gendered geography, and public spaces. Dr. Jyotsna Srivastava holds a Ph.D. in Peace Research from Banaras Hindu University, Varanasi. Her Ph.D. thesis asses existing forms of masculinities producing differential treatment for women migrants. The empirical part explores migrant women’s experiences with masculinity, division of labor, hierarchies, and the social conflict it produces. It was designed to convey the diverse experiences of women migrants in the varying masculinities present, building an alternative experiential knowledge system, stimulating the prevailing framework of gendered migration, and the role of masculinities in social-behavioral patterns during migration. She has qualified for UGC JRF-NET. She was awarded the Linnaeus Palme Scholarship issued by the Swedish International Development Agency during her research. She holds a master's degree in Conflict Management and Development. She has also attended the Summer Intensive Learning Course on: "Religion and Foreign Policy: Investigating Religious Minorities and Human Rights" at McGill University, Montreal, Canada. entries.title: Dr. Ketaki Hanamant Kulkarni
entries.excerpt: Prof. Dr. Ketaki Kulkarni is an esteemed Assistant Professor in the Department of Civil Engineering at MIT WPU, Pune. She graduated from Government College of Engineering, Aurangabad in Civil Engineering in the year 2003, and then completed her M.E in Civil-Water Resources Engineering from Government Engineering College, Aurangabad in 2005. She was selected for a research fellowship in Indian Institute of Technology, Mumbai, and was a research associate there until 2007. Prof. Dr. Kulkarni has been associated with MIT since 2007 and has taught a variety of subjects like Basic Civil and Environmental Engineering, Fluid Mechanics I, II, Momentum Transfer, Hydrology and Water Resources Engineering, Dams and Hydraulic Structures. She earned her doctorate in Civil Engineering from Savitribai Phule Pune University, specializing in Hydraulic Engineering under the guidance of Dr. G. A. Hinge. She has published approximately 4 international journals and 8 international conference papers, including a BEST PAPER AWARD. She is a life member of the Indian Society of Hydraulics (ISH) and the Indian Society of Technical Education (ISTE). She is also serving on the editorial board of LARHYSS journal, which is a Scopus-indexed international journal. Additionally, she is a reviewer for Water Science and Technology - Water Supply, a SCIE/WOS journal rated in the Q2 journal category.

entries.title: Dr. Kumar Abhijeet Raj
entries.excerpt: Dr. Kumar Abhijeet Raj obtained his Ph.D. in Petroleum Engineering from IIT (ISM) Dhanbad. His doctoral research focused on the development of Viscoelastic Surfactant (VES) fracturing fluids for high- temperature reservoirs, integrating key parameters to enhance temperature stability and proppant- carrying capacity. As part of his study, he designed and conducted minifrac tests using specially formulated fracturing fluids and custom-built experimental setups to interpret critical fracturing parameters. He completed both his B.Tech and M.Tech also in Petroleum Engineering from IIT (ISM) Dhanbad.

entries.title: Dr. Kunal Modak
entries.excerpt: Dr. Kunal Modak holds a Ph.D. in Structural Geology from IIT ISM Dhanbad, where he conducted research on the structural geometry, structural mapping, deformation, paleopeizometry, and metamorphism of the Mesoproterozoic Kaladgi Basin. His work provided critical insights into basin evolution and its implications for hydrocarbon and mineral exploration. He also holds an MSc in Geology and an M.Tech in Petroleum Exploration from IIT ISM Dhanbad, equipping him with a strong foundation in both fundamental geological processes and their applications in petroleum systems. He has over 6 years of experience as a scientist under the Ministry of Earth Sciences, where he worked on the prestigious "Scientific Deep Drilling in Koyna Intraplate Seismic Zone" project. His research in this project focused on understanding the mechanisms of reservoir-triggered seismicity through core logging, petrography, microstructural analysis, SEM, and EDS. His research contributions include both single-author and leading-author publications, including a 2022 paper proposing a hypothetical 5 km deep borehole in the Koyna region to study reservoir-triggered seismicity. He also serves as a reviewer for several reputed peer-reviewed journals, contributing to the advancement of geoscientific research.

He has successfully completed various research funding projects. His Research interests include Acoustics, Noise control and its applications, Mechanical System Design, Composite materials, Vibration and condition monitoring, Sustainability and environment. He is also keenly involved in multidisciplinary research. Research Area: Acoustics, Noise control and its applications, Mechanical System Design, Composite materials, Vibration and condition monitoring, Sustainability and environment.

entries.title: Dr. Mandar Bopardikar
entries.excerpt: Molecular biophysics and biochemistry, protein aggregation, NMR spectroscopy, fluorescence methods, and neurodegenerative-disease models.
entries.text: Dr. Mandar Bopardikar Assistant Professor Department of Biosciences and Technology Molecular biophysics and biochemistry, protein aggregation, NMR spectroscopy, fluorescence methods, and neurodegenerative-disease models. Neurodegenerative disorders, characterized by the accumulation of protein aggregates in the human brain, represent a significant threat to human well-being for which no therapeutic treatment is available. Dr. Mandar's research attempts to address this condition by exploring the effect of herbal extracts on protein aggregation. During his doctoral research, he discovered an Ayurvedic extract known as Triphala to be capable of inhibiting the formation of protein aggregates in vitro. Further, the very challenging task of identification of the active ingredient of Triphala was successfully achieved and the mechanistic insights into the inhibition process were obtained. After his PhD, he worked as an industrial R&D scientist on the development of generic equivalent of the following: (a) Peptide hormones involved in controlling blood glucose levels (b) Peptides to treat short bowel syndrome (c) Vitamin supplements. During his short tenure at IISER Berhampur, he was in the pursuit of unravelling the structure and dynamics of complex protein enzymes by NMR spectroscopy. entries.title: Dr. Meera JeevanReddy Chintala
entries.excerpt: Dr. Miss Meera currently works as an assistant professor of civil engineering at Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University, Pune, India, and heads Building Materials & Sustainability (BM&S) team. The researchers of BM&S Group are devoted to elucidating the fundamental science and performance of sustainable building materials. The research mainly focuses on developing green concrete utilizing various wastes through experimentation and implementing AI&ML. Before Joining as an assistant professor, she worked as a research scientist at Shriram Institute for Industrial Research, New Delhi, and developed a strength prediction model for hardened mortar by determining reactive silica content.

entries.title: Dr. Mithila Arvind Biniwale
entries.excerpt: Dr. Mithila Biniwale believes in integrating critical inquiry with practice-based learning, encouraging students to engage with media as both a field of scholarly investigation and creative practice. She is committed to fostering research orientation, critical thinking, and reflective engagement while equipping students with the analytical and production skills required to navigate the contemporary media landscape. Through experiential pedagogies, including documentary filmmaking, field-based learning, podcast production, workshops, and industry interactions, she bridges the gap between theory and practice. By nurturing informed, socially conscious, and innovative media professionals, Dr. Biniwale contributes to MIT World Peace University's vision of progressive transformation through education, innovation, and culture.

entries.title: Dr. Namrata Sathe
entries.excerpt: Dr. Namrata Sathe is an Assistant Professor at the Dadasaheb Phalke International Film Institute, MIT-WPU. She holds a PhD in Media Studies from Southern Illinois University, Carbondale. Her book, The Neoliberal Self in Bollywood: Cinema, Popular Culture, and Identity (Intellect Books, 2024), examines the consequences of unbridled expansion of neoliberal values within India through the lens of popular film and culture. Her scholarship has been published in prominent academic journals including Jump Cut, Journal of Cinema and Media Studies, and New Review of Film and Television Studies , as well as in edited collections on Indian cinema and media. She serves as the Assistant Editor for the SCOPUS-listed academic journal Studies in South Asian Film and Media . Her research interests include feminist media studies, queer studies, sexuality studies, and popular culture.

entries.title: Dr. Nikhilesh Joardar
entries.excerpt: Dr. Nikhilesh Joardar, M.Sc., Ph.D. is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Bioengineering at Dr. Vishwanath D. Karad MIT-World Peace University, Pune. His research spans parasitology, host–pathogen interactions, immunology, and antiparasitic drug development. Dr. Joardar previously worked as a Postdoctoral Research Associate at the Department of Immunology & Microbiology, University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill and Department of Medicine (Division of Infectious Diseases) Washington University School of Medicine in St. Louis, contributing to studies on bacterial, parasitic infections and biomarker discovery. He did his PhD on antifilarial drug development and host-pathogen interaction from the Department of Zoology, Visva Bharati (An Institute of national importance). He has published extensively in leading journals, received multiple national research fellowships, and serves as an Associate Editor for Frontiers in Neglected Tropical Diseases and review editor in Frontiers in Parasitic Diseases. He is a life member of National Environmental Science Academy (NESA), New Delhi and member of American Society for Tropical Medicine and Hygiene (ASTMH). His research interest is focused on exploring redox biology for novel therapeutic interventions in parasitic and infectious diseases and host-pathogen interaction.

Dr. Nithya N. Kutty has completed her doctoral study from IIT Kharagpur. Her doctoral study was focused on dynamics of scent volatiles synthesis, storage and emission in flowers of tuberose, a widely cultivated ornamental plant in Asian countries. During her doctoral study (through CSIR JRF/SRF fellowship), she has also carried out collaborative research work in the area of metabolite profiling from plant endophytes, hairy roots of carrots and studied the effect of gamma rays on floral volatiles. She has also presented her research work in several national and international conferences. She pursued her post-graduation (M Tech) in Agricultural biotechnology from IIT Kharagpur and completed her graduation (B Tech Biotechnology) from JNTU Hyderabad. Her research area of interests includes plant specialized metabolism, plant-microbe interactions, plant volatiles and metabolomics. Presently she is teaching basic and applied courses at undergraduate, postgraduate and doctoral levels. She is a recognized PhD supervisor of the university. entries.excerpt: Bioinorganic Chemistry, Medicinal Chemistry, Molecular Modelling
entries.text: Dr. Poonam Inamdar Assistant Professor Department of Pharmaceutical Sciences Bioinorganic Chemistry, Medicinal Chemistry, Molecular Modelling Dr. Poonam R. Inamdar works in the field of synthetic inorganic chemistry. Her research area interests are the synthesis of the metal complexes and evaluation of their biological studies and biomolecular interactions. She is also working on projects which include protein interaction studies of the organic ligands for the design of potent pharmacophores using spectrometric techniques. Her research focus is mainly an exploration of biomolecular interaction studies of the compounds using UV, FTIR, Conductometry, Cyclic voltammetry, Fluorescence spectroscopy, Viscosity and Circular dichroism studies as well as gel electrophoresis studies of the metal complexes for the assessment of DNA cleavage studies.

She graduated with a First Class degree in Pharmacy from Shivaji University, Kolhapur in 2003, and subsequently completed her M.Pharm and Ph.D. degrees with First Class from Shivaji University Kolhapur and Savitribai Phule Pune University respectively. Dr. Adsule has 16 years of experience in teaching and 6 years of research experience. She is currently serving as an Assistant Professor at Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University, School of Pharmacy, Pune. Dr. Adsule has published numerous research papers in national and international journals and is a life member of APTI and IPA. She has also received research grants and awards for her contributions to the field. entries.excerpt: Dr. Prajkta Bhide is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Public Health at MIT-WPU. She completed her Masters in Health Science degree from Savitribai Phule Pune University (formerly known as University of Pune). She holds a doctoral degree (PhD in Health Science) from Savitribai Phule Pune University. Her doctoral work explored the epidemiology of birth defects / congenital disorders in India and their larger public health impact. She areas of expertise include conducting systematic reviews and meta-analyses, and quantitative data analyses. Her areas of interest include birth defects, maternal and child health, epidemiology and health economics.

entries.text: Dr. Preethi Sunkara Assistant Professor Department of Biosciences and Technology Plant developmental biology, plant–pathogen interactions, combined biotic and abiotic stress, plant genetics, and molecular biology. Dr. Preethi Sunkara is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Biosciences and Technology, MIT-WPU. She is a Molecular and Developmental Plant Biologist who earned her Ph.D. from the Indian Institute of Science (IISc), Bangalore. Her doctoral research focused on the biochemical and genetic characterization of TCP3 and TCP4 transcription factors in Arabidopsis thaliana, specifically investigating their antagonistic roles in leaf development and growth. Her expertise spans advanced plant genetics, including EMS mutagenesis, ChIP assays, and confocal microscopy, Protein interactions, Molecular biology. An accomplished researcher, Preethi has published in journals such as PLoS Genetics and Plant Physiology. She received the second prize for best poster at the 29th International Conference on Arabidopsis Research (ICAR-2018) in Finland. Beyond her primary research, she has supervised numerous Masters and undergraduate student projects and possesses a strong background in biotechnology and Plant developmental biology. Her teaching interests include Molecular Biology, Genetic Engineering, Genetics and applied Biotechnology . : Environmental Biotechnology, Biochemistry, M

entries.excerpt: Metal-oxide and nanocomposite thick and thin films for gas sensing.
entries.text: Dr. Raju Shivaji Ingale Assistant Professor Department of Physics Metal-oxide and nanocomposite thick and thin films for gas sensing. Dr. Raju Shivaji Ingale has over 15 years of experience as an academician, including 11 years as an Assistant Professor of Physics at MIT-WPU, Pune. He holds a Ph.D. in Nanotechnology from the Department of Physics, PSGVPM ACS College Shahada, Kavayitri Bahinabai Chaudhari North Maharashtra University, Jalgaon, and M.Sc. in Physics from prestigious Fergusson College Pune, affiliated with Savitribai Phule Pune University. Dr. Ingale has qualified both the SET and GATE examinations established himself as a dedicated researcher and academician. His expertise lies in the fabrication, characterization, and design of cost-effective doped metal oxide and nanocomposite thick and thin films for gas sensing applications. He has authored 7 international research papers published in reputed journals and holds two granted international patents underscoring his innovative approach to research. Beyond academia, he is actively involved in creating impactful technological solutions, such as a women’s safety device, showcasing his commitment to addressing societal challenges through innovation.

entries.title: Dr. Saloni Kumar

Her academic interests focus on integrating psychological theory with practical applications to foster holistic learning and personal growth. Passionate about mentoring students, she strives to bridge classroom learning with real-world experiences through innovative teaching approaches and experiential learning. entries.title: Dr. Sandeep Namdev Dhikale
entries.excerpt: Dr. Sandeep Dhikale, also known as Sandeepsagar, embodies a distinctive confluence of the traditional Gurukul system and modern educational paradigms. His academic pursuits span a spectrum of disciplines, including Sanskrit, Peace Studies, Indology, and Meditation. Dr. Sandeep has actively contributed to academia by presenting over 22 research papers at various international, national, and state-level conferences, reflecting his interdisciplinary approach and commitment to scholarly excellence. He is also the author of three acclaimed books: ‘Understanding Peace’; ‘Dimensions of Peace’; ‘Indian Knowledge Systems: A Treasure Trove of the Way of Holistic and Sustainable Life’ These works reflect his deep engagement with the themes of peace, holistic living, and the rich heritage of Indian knowledge traditions. He is currently serving as an Assistant Professor in Peace Studies at the School of Business and Management, MIT World Peace University, Pune.

entries.title: Dr. Savitri Nathrao Jadhav
entries.department: Department of Electrical and Electronics Engineering
entries.excerpt: Dr. Savitri N. Jadhav is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Electrical and Electronics Engineering with over 18+ years of academic and research experience. She completed her Ph.D. from Savitribai Phule Pune University in 2022. Handwritten MODI character recognition was her topic of research. Her research interests span Artificial Intelligence & Machine Learning (AIML), Embedded AI, Image Processing, Pattern Recognition, and Edge AI. She has contributed extensively to interdisciplinary domains including UAV-based flood detection, anomaly detection using embedded AI, and intelligent agricultural systems. She has published numerous research papers in reputed journals and conferences and holds multiple Indian patents in areas such as crop damage assessment, non-invasive glucose monitoring, and UV-C disinfection systems. She is actively involved in consultancy projects with industry partners like KPIT Technologies and Bharat Flow Analytics, focusing on real-world AI and embedded system applications. A professional member of IEEE and associated with the Geoscience and Remote Sensing Society, she is committed to advancing research, innovation, and student mentorship in emerging technologies.

entries.title: Dr. Shalaka Patil
entries.excerpt: Genome stability in cancer, DNA damage and repair, cancer epigenetics, phase separation, and optogenetic tools.
entries.text: Dr. Shalaka Patil Assistant Professor Department of Biosciences and Technology Genome stability in cancer, DNA damage and repair, cancer epigenetics, phase separation, and optogenetic tools. Dr. Shalaka Patil is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Biosciences and Technology, MIT-WPU, Pune. She is a dedicated and passionate researcher with a PhD in Biology from the Indian Institute of Science Education and Research (IISER), Pune, under the supervision of Prof. Kundan Sengupta. She holds university rank 1 for her M.Sc. in microbiology from SPPU. She has qualified CSIR-UGC NET-JRF with AIR 22. Her research area is genome biology and chromosomal instability. Her doctoral work focused on understanding the role of the nuclear envelope in maintaining the genomic stability of cancer cells. After her PhD, she worked as a Senior Project Associate at IISER Pune on a project to develop a cost-effective FISH kit for detecting HER2 amplification in breast cancer patient samples. Later, she joined the CNRS Institute of Human Genetics, Montpellier, France, for her postdoctoral research with Dr. Marcel Mechali. Her post-doctoral work was focused on developing an in vivo system to examine the phase separation of the initiation of DNA replication. Through her work, she aims to contribute to advancements in medical research, particularly developing strategies for the early diagnosis of can

entries.text: Dr. Sharbani Kaushik Program Director Department of Bioengineering Biomaterials, Bioenergy, Electrochemical devices, Biosensors, Microbial Electrolysis, CO2 conversion Dr. Sharbani Kaushik is a renowned biotechnology researcher with expertise in bioenergy, bioelectronics, and membrane proteins. She completed her Ph.D. in the School of Energy Science and Engineering from IIT Guwahati. Her work in renewable energy and bioanalytical applications includes pioneering research on microbial fuel cells and biofuel cell biosensing devices. As a postdoctoral researcher at The Ohio State University, USA, she made significant contributions to chemistry and biochemistry, especially in membrane proteins, synthetic biology and drug discovery, resulting in multiple high-impact publications. She has also served as an Assistant Professor at the Regional Institute of Science and Technology in Meghalaya, where she introduced new courses and mentored students in biosensors and biomedical instrumentation.

entries.title: Dr. Shraddha Kulkarni
entries.excerpt: Dr. Shraddha Kulkarni is a passionate academician with a wealth of 17 years of experience in engineering teaching and research in Biotechnology. Her experience includes teaching interdisciplinary and application oriented courses like Fermentation Technology, Bioprocess Engineering, Biothermodynamics, Bioseparation Engineering, Bioreaction Engineering etc. She has always emphasized on experiential learning with hands-on training of the students to make them industry ready. she has worked for development of syllabus for B.Tech Biotechnology, NAAC and NBA accreditation of the programs, development of laboratory courses etc. Beyond the classroom, Dr. Kulkarni is deeply engaged in cutting-edge research endeavors. She has completed her Ph.D from Department of Technology, SPPU Pune. Her areas of expertise span across diverse domains within Bioengineering, encompassing fermentation technology, Bioprocess Design, proteomic technology, computational studies of biomolecules. Through her research, she endeavors to address the development of environmentally friendly surfactant products and their application in various fields. Committed to the advancement of her field, Dr. Kulkarni is an author, with numerous publications in esteemed journals and conferences. She has fetched research grants from agencies like Savitribai Phule Pune University, Institution of Engineers, etc. As an Assistant Professor at MIT World Peace University, Dr. Kulkarni embodies the institution's ethos of promoting peace through education and research. Her unwavering commitment to excellence, coupled with her passion for fostering innovation, makes her a valued member of the academic community, dedicated to shaping the engineers of tomorrow and contributing to a brighter, more sustainable world.

She has completed her Ph.D from Department of Technology, SPPU Pune. Her areas of expertise span across diverse domains within Bioengineering, encompassing fermentation technology, Bioprocess Design, proteomic technology, computational studies of biomolecules. Through her research, she endeavors to address the development of environmentally friendly surfactant products and their application in various fields. entries.title: Dr. Shreya Bera
entries.excerpt: Dr. Shreya Bera is an Assistant Professor of English at Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University. She completed her PhD in Literary Studies from the University of Szeged in January 2023 under the prestigious Stipendium Hungaricum Scholarship Programme (2018-2023). She attended multiple international conferences such as the 14th Biennial Conference of the Hungarian Society for the Study of English (HUSSE) in Hungary (2019), 11th Brno International Conference, Czech Republic, ESSE Doctoral Symposium in France (2020), Narratives and Self Ambivalence symposium, SAS, London in 2021 and more. She also co-edited a book titled Critical Essays on Multicultural India: Exploring Inclusiveness and New Humanities published in 2017. She has also authored articles and book reviews in peer-reviewed, and Scopus indexed journals such as Americana e-journal, British and American studies (B.A.S.) journal, The ESSE Messenger etc. She is an established author of two books published in the years of 2017 and 2025.

entries.title: Dr. Shubhangi Rastogi
entries.excerpt: Bioethanol, Biocatalyst, lignocellulosic residues, probiotics, and yogurts.
entries.text: Dr. Shubhangi Rastogi Assistant Professor Department of Bioengineering Bioethanol, Biocatalyst, lignocellulosic residues, probiotics, and yogurts. Dr. Shubhangi Rastogi is an experienced academician and researcher with a background in Microbiology and Industrial Biotechnology. She holds a Master of Science in Biotechnology and a Ph. D. in Microbiology from Panjab University, Chandigarh. With over 2 years of industrial and 1.5 years of research experience, her expertise is in bioprocess: biocatalyst, microbial cultures, 2-G bio-ethanol production, probiotic development, and in-vitro drug development. She teaches bioprocess engineering and industrial biotechnology at the undergraduate level. Dr. Rastogi has been awarded with a DBT-BIRAC E-YUVA scale-up project on bioethanol production in 2022. Dr. Rastogi’s previous research focused on the scale-up of biocatalyst cocktail production and utilization in the biological pretreatment of lignocellulosic residues for bioethanol production. Her present research interests include the scale-up of biocatalysts, specifically ligno-hemicellulases, biological pretreatment of lignocelluloses with a major focus on rice straw, bioethanol production, and development of probiotics and yogurts for exploring gut gut-brain axis.

entries.title: Dr. Soubir Das
entries.excerpt: Dr. Soubir Das holds a Ph.D. in Petroleum Engineering from IIT (ISM) Dhanbad, specializing in the development of clay-free drilling fluid systems for gas hydrate-bearing formations. His research focused on formulating low-dosage hydrate inhibitors (LDHIs) for water-based, non-damaging polymeric drilling fluids, ensuring enhanced performance and minimal reservoir damage. With expertise in drilling technology, drilling fluids, and LDHI synthesis, Dr. Das has extensive experience with advanced analytical instruments such as FT-IR, TGA-DTA-DSC, Particle Size Analyzer, Zeta Potential Analyzer, and high- pressure dynamic flow-loop systems (up to 10,000 psi). He holds an MTech. Petroleum Engineering and B.E. Petroleum and Offshore Engineering from IIT(ISM) Dhanbad and AMET University Chennai as well. He has collaborated with the Gas Hydrate Research & Technology Centre (GHRTC), ONGC, Panvel, Navi Mumbai applying theoretical insights to industry challenges during his Ph.D. Dr. Das has authored multiple SCI-indexed journal publications and presented at national and international conferences. He is skilled in both independent research and multidisciplinary teamwork, contributing to advancements in petroleum engineering.

entries.title: Dr. Suchitra Renguntwar
entries.excerpt: Dr. Suchitra Renguntwar (Ajgaonkar) is an Assistant Professor of Psychology in the Department of Liberal Arts at MIT WPU, teaching industrial psychology, psychological assessment in HRD and counselling, and health psychology. She holds a Ph.D. in management and a master’s degree in psychology and is qualified for UGC NET and SET in psychology. She has over six years of experience teaching MBA students and eight years of industry experience. Her empirical research focuses on workforce agility in the information technology industry, learning mechanisms and TQM in the manufacturing industry, and resilience and women empowerment in social enterprises. Her research papers are published in ABDC (B) category journals – International Journal of Organizational Analysis and TQM Journal. Her research interests include socio-cognitive elements in psychology, nudge theory, and collaborative research on the application of psychology to management. She has expertise in mixed-method research, systematic literature review, and qualitative research methods.

entries.department: Department of Wellness and Yogic Science
entries.excerpt: Dr. Suman Rawat is serving as an Assistant Professor in the Department of Wellness and Yogic Science at the School of Health Sciences and Technology, MIT-WPU. She is passionate about promoting holistic health and wellness through yoga education and practices. With experience in teaching yogic philosophy and therapeutic applications of yoga, she brings both academic depth and practical insight to her role. Dr. Rawat holds a Ph.D. in Yoga from Shoolini University and has qualified for the UGC-NET (Yoga). Her academic qualifications also include an M.Sc. in Yoga Therapy from Manipal Academy of Higher Education (MAHE) and a B.Sc. in Yoga Science from Morarji Desai National Institute of Yoga (MDNIY). She has also published research papers in the field of yoga and wellness. Before joining MIT-WPU, she was the Academic and NAAC Co-ordinator at Shoolini University.

Synthetic medicinal chemistry, in vitro, in vivo assays, solubility enhancement for poorly soluble drugs Dr. Rayala Swetha is a medicinal chemist specializing in drug discovery for neurodegenerative diseases, with a particular focus on Alzheimer’s disease. Her research integrates computational design, chemical synthesis, and biological evaluation across enzyme assays, cell-based systems, and animal models to identify and design novel therapeutic molecules.

entries.title: Dr. Tanya Kumar
entries.excerpt: Dr. Tanya Kumar is an Assistant Professor at the School of Business, MIT World Peace University, where she is recognized for her dynamic teaching approach and strong research orientation. With over four years of academic experience in marketing and finance, she brings conceptual clarity, industry relevance, and learner-centric pedagogy into the classroom. Her teaching interests span Marketing Management, Strategic Management, and Consumer Behaviour, with a focus on developing analytical thinking and practical decision-making skills among students. Beyond teaching, she plays an active role in academic administration and institutional development, contributing to curriculum design, NAAC documentation, and the coordination of Faculty Development Programmes (FDPs). She is a prolific researcher with publications in reputed Scopus-, Web of Science-, and ABDC-listed journals, and has authored several international book chapters with globally renowned publishers including IGI Global, Emerald, Routledge, and Wiley. Driven by a passion for academic excellence, leadership, and innovation, Dr. Tanya is committed to advancing impactful research and nurturing future-ready management professionals through quality education and scholarly engagement.

entries.title: Dr. Vaishali Namdevrao Choudhari
entries.excerpt: Psychology, Mental health
entries.text: Dr. Vaishali Namdevrao Choudhari Assistant Professor Department of Liberal Arts Psychology, Mental health Dr. Vaishali Choudhari is a scholar with UGC NET and PhD in Psychology. Her research interests include workplace conflict, positive psychology, mental health, and gender differences. She has authored several articles in reputed journals such as the Indian Journal of Psychology and Education, International Journal of Indian Psychology, and Indian Journal of Natural Sciences. Dr. Choudhari's work includes a systematic review of prenatal mental health in India, a comparative investigation of personality traits and gender differences among management students, and a study on haptic memory in deaf adolescents. She is a dedicated researcher and has made significant contributions to the field of psychology. Research Areas Psychology, Mental health Publications Positivity at Work-Place: A Comparative Investigation & An Inevitable Work-Place Conflict: An Overview Google Scholar link: Google Scholar

entries.excerpt: Microfinance, Financial Inclusion, Corporate Finance
entries.text: Dr. Vedashree Tushar Mali Assistant Professor Department of Business Microfinance, Financial Inclusion, Corporate Finance Dr. Vedashree Mali is an Assistant Professor at the MIT-WPU School of Business (WPU-SOB) in Pune, India. She completed her Ph.D. in Finance and an MBA in Finance from Savitribai Phule Pune University. At WPU-SOB, she teaches courses such as Corporate Finance, Financial Modeling, and Corporate Valuation. Her research interests include Microfinance, Financial Inclusion, and Corporate Finance. With her expertise in these areas, she has contributed to multiple research publications and conference presentations. Dr. Vedashree has also participated in various workshops, seminars, and training programs related to Finance and Financial Modeling. Case on Financial Modeling, Demonetization: A step towards modified India

entries.title: Dr. Vibha Utpal Patel
entries.department: Department of Electrical and Electronics Engineering
entries.excerpt: wireless communication, Digital communication, Neural Networks, Machine Learning, Data Science, Electromagnetics and Antennas, and Digital signal processing.
entries.text: Dr. Vibha Utpal Patel Assistant Professor Department of Electrical and Electronics Engineering wireless communication, Digital communication, Neural Networks, Machine Learning, Data Science, Electromagnetics and Antennas, and Digital signal processing. Dr. Vibha Patel received her Ph.D. in Electronics & Communication from Gujarat Technological University in 2023. With over 12 years of teaching experience, she has made notable contributions to her field. Her research interests span wireless communication, digital communication, neural networks, machine learning, data science, electromagnetics and antennas, and digital signal processing. Dr. Patel's work has resulted in the grant of one Australian patent and one South African patent in her name. She has worked on various noteworthy projects, such as the TDMA-based data logger for AWS, moisture sensor using patch antenna, clustering analysis on world health data, and job recommender system, among others. 1. entries.title: Dr. Vikas Keshav Jadhav
entries.excerpt: Political Sociology, Social Exclusion/Marginalization and Politics of Recognition, Inclusion and Justice; Development Sociology, Critical Phenomenology; Critical Caste Studies; and Sociology of Caste, Tribe and Inter-subjectivity with special reference to Dalit, Adivasis and De-notified and Nomadic Tribes of India.
entries.text: Dr. Vikas Keshav Jadhav Assistant Professor Department of Liberal Arts Political Sociology, Social Exclusion/Marginalization and Politics of Recognition, Inclusion and Justice; Development Sociology, Critical Phenomenology; Critical Caste Studies; and Sociology of Caste, Tribe and Inter-subjectivity with special reference to Dalit, Adivasis and De-notified and Nomadic Tribes of India. Dr. Vikas is a Ph.D. in Sociology from the Centre for the Study of Social Systems, Jawaharlal Nehru University, New Delhi. He is also a trained development sector professional from Tata Institute of Social Sciences, Mumbai, having an experience of field as well as working with Public Sector Unit – NTPC Limited. He employs an interdisciplinary approach and mixed- method framework to study the politics of marginalization in India. His work engages in examining the social, political and historical factors that accentuate the processes of alterity construction, stigmatization, exclusion, and how different social groups negotiate and counter diverse structural constraints.

entries.title: Dr. Vinayak Sudhakar Wadgaonkar
entries.excerpt: Dr. Vinayak S. Wadgaonkar has completed his doctoral study from L.I.T. nagpur. His doctoral study was focused on Studies on Removal of Heavy Metals By Low Cost Adsorbents. During his doctoral study, he has also carried out collaborative research work in the area of waste water treatment and environmental engineering. He has also presented her research work in several national and international conferences. He pursued his post-graduation (M. E.) in Chemical Engineering from Shivaji University and completed his graduation (B.E) in Chemical Engineering from Amravati University, Amravati. His research area of interests includes natural gas engineering, enhanced oil recovery, flow assurance, waste water treatment, hydrogen production and environmental engineering. Presently he is teaching basic and applied courses at undergraduate, post graduate. He is also recognized Phd guide in Petroleum and Chemical Engineering. He also mentors students for their research project.

entries.title: Dr. Vinod Sartape
entries.excerpt: Migration and Diaspora, Social Exclusion, Anthropology of Caste, Religion, Social Movements
entries.text: Dr. Vinod Sartape Assistant Professor(Sociology) Department of Liberal Arts Migration and Diaspora, Social Exclusion, Anthropology of Caste, Religion, Social Movements Dr Vinod Sartape is Assistant Professor of Sociology in the Department of Liberal Arts, MIT-World Peace University, Pune. He has obtained a doctorate in Sociology and Social Anthropology from Central European University, Vienna, Austria. His research areas include Migration and Diaspora, Social Exclusion, Anthropology of Caste, Religion, Globalisation, and Social Movements in India and the Globe. Dr Vinod has an MPhil in Sociology from Jawaharlal Nehru University, New Delhi and an MA in Social Work from Tata Institute of Social Sciences, Mumbai. He is qualified the NET-JRF in Sociology. Prior to joining MIT-WPU, Vinod has taught Sociology for three years at the Babes-Bolyai University, Romania

entries.title: Dr. Vivek Patel
entries.excerpt: Crashworthiness, Impact analysis, Design, Non-linear analysis, FEA
entries.text: Dr. Vivek Patel Assistant Professor Department of Mechanical Engineering Crashworthiness, Impact analysis, Design, Non-linear analysis, FEA An Assistant Professor in the School of Engineering and Technology, Dr. Vivek Patel's research interests center around nonlinear structural analysis with a particular focus on studying the impact behavior of tubular components in the automobile industry. He is an expert in modeling and simulation of engineering parts using software such as AutoCAD, LS-Dyna, Ansys, and Abaqus. In addition, he has hands-on experience with experimental set-ups such as UTM, Hardness tester, and Ultrasonic assisted casting process. With his technical competency and expertise, he is capable of confirming the statistics of experimental tests with accuracy. 1. entries.excerpt: Dr. Waseem Khan is currently working as an Assistant Professor in the School of Business at Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University, Pune. He has over six years of diverse experience across academia, research, and industry, having been associated with reputed institutions such as Aligarh Muslim University, Jamia Hamdard, GLA University (Mathura), and the Indian Institute of Management, Lucknow, as well as with ICICI Bank Ltd. He holds a master’s degree and a Ph.D. in Agricultural Economics and Business Management from Aligarh Muslim University and is also qualified in the ASRB-NET in both Agricultural Business Management and Agricultural Economics.

entries.title: Facilities
entries.excerpt: Teaching and research facilities in the Department of Biosciences and Technology at MIT-WPU.
entries.text: Facilities · Department of Biosciences and Technology Facilities Teaching and research facilities in the Department of Biosciences and Technology at MIT-WPU. Biosciences & Technology Facilities 01 Revvity – MIT-WPU Centre of Excellence for Bioimaging Technologies and Innovation Confocal microscopy, flow cytometry, live-animal imaging, microfluidics, gene extraction, and advanced imaging software. 02 Cell culture and tissue engineering Animal-cell culture, tissue engineering, disease models, and regenerative-biology research. 03 Molecular biology, bioanalytics and bioinformatics Molecular and bioanalytical workflows, genomics, and computational bioscience. 04 Microbial and plant biotechnology Microbial culture, bioprocessing, plant biotechnology, and applied sustainable bioscience. Shared infrastructure MIT-WPU Research Facilities Significant shared research instruments and faculty contacts are maintained separately from routine teaching equipment. Central facilities

entries.title: Facilities
entries.excerpt: Teaching and research facilities in the Department of Chemistry at MIT-WPU.
entries.text: Facilities · Department of Chemistry Facilities Teaching and research facilities in the Department of Chemistry at MIT-WPU. Chemistry Facilities 01 Chemical synthesis and analysis Laboratory teaching covers organic, inorganic, physical, and analytical chemistry, instrumental methods, and spectroscopic structure determination. 02 Polymer processing and characterisation Industrial Polymer Chemistry covers polymer synthesis, processing, raw-material analysis, coatings, testing, and characterisation. Shared infrastructure MIT-WPU Research Facilities Significant shared research instruments and faculty contacts are maintained separately from routine teaching equipment. Central facilities

entries.title: Garima Chaudhary
entries.excerpt: Garima Chaudhary is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Visual Arts at the School of Design, MIT World Peace University (MIT-WPU). She holds Bachelor’s, Master’s, and PhD degrees in Fine Arts from Aligarh Muslim University, reflecting a strong academic foundation. With over two years of experience in Visual Arts education, Garima combines academic rigor with practical expertise. Her professional skills encompass research, drawing, painting, branding, print and digital media, semiotics, and photography, with a particular research interest in photography, art, and design. In her teaching, she integrates creative vision with structured project management, enabling her to mentor students effectively. This approach helps students develop critical thinking and robust art skills, preparing them for diverse creative challenges. Garima’s commitment to both academic excellence and hands-on learning fosters a dynamic and supportive environment for aspiring artists and practitioners.

entries.title: Harshit Pratap Singh
entries.excerpt: Harshit is an educator and designer, specializing in areas of research, graphic design, and film-making. He holds a UGC-NET qualification, and has completed an M.A. in Education from Tata Institute of Social Sciences, Mumbai and a Bachelor of Design from Indian Institute of Technology, Bombay. Before joining the Department of Design at MIT World Peace University as an Assistant Professor, he has worked with multiple NGOs including Mantra4Change, Simple Education Foundation, Myna Mahila Foundation in varying capacities of researcher, designer, and educator.
entries.text: Harshit Pratap Singh Assistant Professor Department of Design Harshit is an educator and designer, specializing in areas of research, graphic design, and film-making. He holds a UGC-NET qualification, and has completed an M.A. in Education from Tata Institute of Social Sciences, Mumbai and a Bachelor of Design from Indian Institute of Technology, Bombay. Before joining the Department of Design at MIT World Peace University as an Assistant Professor, he has worked with multiple NGOs including Mantra4Change, Simple Education Foundation, Myna Mahila Foundation in varying capacities of researcher, designer, and educator.

entries.text: Heramb Chintamani Phadake Assistant Professor Department of Mechanical Engineering Air conditioning, Energy optimization Prof. Heramb Phadake is a highly accomplished Assistant Professor with a diverse background in Mechanical Engineering. With a 20+ years of experience, he has spent 20 years working in the industry and 5 years teaching. Prof. Phadake holds a Ph.D. in Mechanical Engineering (currently pursuing), an M.Tech. in Energy Engineering - Design, and a B.E. in Mechanical Engineering. He specializes in Energy Engineering, Thermal Engineering, and Automotive Technology, and is a member of the Society of Automotive Engineering (SAE). Prof. Phadake's strengths lie in process planning and execution, and he has gained extensive coordination experience while working on infrastructure projects for over 20 years.

entries.title: Himangi Tanmay Neve
entries.excerpt: Polymers, Biomaterials, Biodegradation, Waste management and valorisation, Life Cycle Assessment
entries.text: Himangi Tanmay Neve Assistant Professor Department of Bioengineering Polymers, Biomaterials, Biodegradation, Waste management and valorisation, Life Cycle Assessment Himangi Neve is an Assistant Professor in the Bioengineering Department of Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University in Pune, India. She has a master’s degree in polymer engineering and is now pursuing a PhD in chemical engineering. She has 11 years of combined job experience in industry and academia. Her research focuses on developing more effective and efficient bioconversion technologies for converting agricultural waste into high-value items, as well as evaluating the significance of life cycle assessment in processes ranging from waste collection to product distribution. She has produced over 5 research articles in international journals and 3 book chapters, and has garnered numerous accolades, including two Best Paper awards at international conferences and a runner-up team at the MITWPU Hackathon. She also won a Rs. 3 lakh research grant from MITWPU, as well as Rs. 1 lakh in the Hackathon. Himangi has also completed a number of waste management consulting projects for national and multinational companies. She has also published an Indian patent. In addition to her teaching and research responsibilities, Himangi is the department’s IQAC, Exam, Infrastructure, and Rural Immersion Coordinator. 1. Potdar P

entries.title: Maisam Ali
entries.excerpt: Maisam Ali is an independent filmmaker with expertise in both short and feature films. He is a graduate of the Film and Television Institute of India (FTII), majoring in Film Direction. His short films have been exhibited and honoured in France, the United States, the United Arab Emirates, and India. His feature film In Retreat was the first Indian film to be included in the ACID sidebar section at the Cannes Film Festival 2024, marking a 32-year milestone. The film was screened at the Busan International Film Festival, Sao Paulo Film Festival, Galway Film Festival, Moscow International Film Festival, and Mumbai Film Festival, among others. In addition, he has been teaching filmmaking at several institutions in India. He is presently overseeing Direction practices at Dadasaheb International Film School in the position of Assistant Professor.
