entries.title: Manisha Mohan Wani
entries.department: Department of Electrical and Electronics Engineering
entries.excerpt: Electrical Power systems
entries.text: Manisha Mohan Wani Assistant Professor Department of Electrical and Electronics Engineering Electrical Power systems Prof. Manisha Mohan Wani is an accomplished electrical engineer and educator with over 11 years of teaching experience. She graduated with a degree in Electrical Engineering in 2004 from Pune Vidyrthi Gruha’s College of Engineering, Pune University, and completed her ME in Electrical Power Systems in 2012 from the same institution. Since August 1, 2007, Prof. Wani has been working at MIT-WPU, where she has taken on a range of academic and administrative responsibilities. She is a permanent member of the Institution of Engineers, India and has served as a class teacher, ISO & NBA coordinator of CEES department, felicitation committee head of CEES department, and ARC officer for FE, direct second year, and ME admissions, among other duties. Additionally, Prof. Wani has been a paper setting committee member at SPPU and MIT-WPU and a university flying squad member at MIT-WPU. Her expertise in electrical engineering and her dedication to teaching have made her an asset to the academic community.

entries.title: Mr. Prathamesh Sonawani
entries.excerpt: Prathamesh Sonawani believes teaching is about awakening perception, not just delivering information. In his classes, he encourages students to listen, observe, and think critically about sound, performance, and storytelling as lived experiences, not just technical subjects. His motivation comes from seeing learners discover their own artistic voice and confidence. He blends practical industry insight with creative exploration, so students are prepared for both professional realities and meaningful expression. At MIT-WPU, he hopes to contribute to an environment where artistic skill, cultural awareness, and emotional intelligence grow together in thoughtful, socially aware creators.

entries.excerpt: Stress Analysis, Failure Analysis, Corrosion and Wear Analysis, Mechanical Engineering Design, Mechanical Vibrations, Biomechanics, Energy Harvesting, Nano technology
entries.text: Nitinkumar Raghunath Anekar Assistant Professor Department of Mechanical Engineering Stress Analysis, Failure Analysis, Corrosion and Wear Analysis, Mechanical Engineering Design, Mechanical Vibrations, Biomechanics, Energy Harvesting, Nano technology Nitinkumar Anekar is a highly skilled and experienced Mechanical Engineer with expertise in Manufacturing Techniques, Nano technology, Nano energy, Vibration Analysis, Mechanism and Synthesis, Design Engineering and Failure Analysis. Currently, he is an Assistant Professor at the School of Mechanical Engineering at Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University in Pune, India. He has conducted extensive research in the field of Mechanical Engineering and has published several research papers in renowned international journals. He is also an accomplished lecturer and has delivered talks on various subjects related to Mechanical Engineering. In addition to his academic pursuits, Nitinkumar maintains an online presence through his website nitinanekar.wixsite.com/nitinkumar-anekar, which showcases his research and academic achievements. 1. entries.title: NSRTC 2025
entries.excerpt: A national meeting of scientists, technologists, researchers, policy leaders, and students organised around research priorities for Viksit Bharat.
entries.text: NSRTC 2025 NSRTC 2025 A national meeting of scientists, technologists, researchers, policy leaders, and students organised around research priorities for Viksit Bharat. Conference NSRTC 2025 Science, innovation, and the vision for Viksit Bharat Date 18 July 2025 · three-day conference Location MIT World Peace University, Pune Format Conference Status Past event Official MIT-WPU event report ↗ Overview A national meeting of scientists, technologists, researchers, policy leaders, and students organised around research priorities for Viksit Bharat. The programme opened with plenary sessions on Nobel Prize-recognised work in medicine, chemistry, physics, and engineering, followed by specialist talks and a formal inaugural session. Technical sessions and round tables connected fundamental research, applied science, technology development, and questions of national capacity. Themes Artificial intelligence Advanced materials and processing Digital transformation, energy, and sustainability Healthcare, pharmaceutical science, and biotechnology Programme Special talks Ashutosh Sharma spoke on science and technology for Viksit Bharat; Arindam Ghosh on materials for quantum technologies; and Ajit Kembhavi on the life and scientific journey of Jayant Vishnu Narlikar. Scientific programme Parallel sessions covered artificial intelligence, materials and devices, energy and sustainability, healthcare, pharmaceuticals, and biotechnology, with posters and round-table discussions.

entries.title: NSRTC 2026
entries.excerpt: Scientists, researchers, industry leaders, policymakers, innovators, and students met for interdisciplinary discussions on emerging research and technology.
entries.text: NSRTC 2026 NSRTC 2026 Scientists, researchers, industry leaders, policymakers, innovators, and students met for interdisciplinary discussions on emerging research and technology. Conference NSRTC 2026 A three-day conclave advancing science, technology, and innovation Date 17–19 July 2026 Location MIT World Peace University, Pune Format Conference Status Past event Official MIT-WPU event report ↗ Overview Scientists, researchers, industry leaders, policymakers, innovators, and students met for interdisciplinary discussions on emerging research and technology. The programme combined invited talks and parallel technical sessions with round-table discussions, poster presentations, project exhibitions, and a Scholars-to-Scientists Connect initiative. The conference brought together contributors from universities, national laboratories, research institutes, industry, and public organisations. Themes Reimagining the future with artificial intelligence Materials, energy, and sustainability Emergent device technologies New biology and health sciences Programme Day 1 Opening sessions considered major scientific and engineering prizes, followed by the inaugural programme, research and entrepreneurship awards, keynote addresses, and a lecture on national wealth through science and technology. Days 2–3 Four parallel research tracks covered artificial intelligence, sustainable energy, advanced materials, semiconductors, health technologies, biotechnology, quantum technologies, and indigenous innovation.

entries.title: Pallavi Choudhary
entries.excerpt: Pallavi Choudhary is a visual artist, designer and film maker and the current Program Co-ordinator of Animation and VFX Design. She has over 6 years of work experience in the design industry and academia in India and abroad. She currently works at the intersection of Social Design, Visual Communication and storytelling.

entries.title: Pallavi Rajwade
entries.excerpt: Brand Perception and Packaging Visuals
entries.text: Pallavi Rajwade Assistant Professor, Program Co-ordinator BFA & MFA (Applied Arts) Department of Visual Arts Brand Perception and Packaging Visuals Ms. Pallavi Rajwade is passionate about communication design and believes that curiosity and creativity make life fun and enriched. She has received her formal education in Applied Arts along with a Post Graduate Diploma in Public Relations & Advertising. She has worked as a Visualiser and Graphic Designer for over 15 years. in India as well as abroad. She has almost 5+ years of enriching experience in Academics as BFA and MFA Program Coordinator for AY 2023-2024. Also, she is currently pursuing PhD from Symbiosis International University. Currently, she is teaching courses such as Advertising, Branding, and Visualisation and has mentored final-year students for Visual Communication Design projects for the last 2 years at MIT WPU Department of Visual Arts, School of Design.

entries.title: Parul Ghole
entries.excerpt: Parul Ghole is a distinguished Visual Artist with extensive experience in academia and design training. She has served as an Assistant Professor at JNAFAU(4 years), Hyderabad, and Bharati Vidyapeeth, Pune(1 year), and is currently (since jan 2023 )a faculty member at MIT-WPU,Pune, where her research interests include Illustration, Visualization, and Fundamentals of Design. Additionally, since 2019, she has contributed as a Senior Design Trainer at MAD School in Hyderabad. Her diverse roles highlight her commitment to fostering artistic talent and design principles across various educational institutions.

entries.title: Piyush Shaha
entries.excerpt: Mr. Piyush Shaha is an accomplished academician with extensive experience in the fields of Statistics, Operations Management, and Business Administration. He holds multiple postgraduate degrees, including an M.Sc. in Statistics, M.B.A. in Operations, and M.Com. in Business Administration, along with a B.Sc. in Statistics. He has also qualified the SET (State Eligibility Test) in Mathematical Sciences and is currently pursuing a Ph.D. in Statistics.
entries.text: Piyush Shaha Assistant Professor Department of Business Mr. Piyush Shaha is an accomplished academician with extensive experience in the fields of Statistics, Operations Management, and Business Administration. He holds multiple postgraduate degrees, including an M.Sc. in Statistics, M.B.A. in Operations, and M.Com. in Business Administration, along with a B.Sc. in Statistics. He has also qualified the SET (State Eligibility Test) in Mathematical Sciences and is currently pursuing a Ph.D. in Statistics.

entries.title: Prajakta Balchandra Khanwalkar
entries.excerpt: Industrial Design, Visual Ergonomics, Design Eductaion, Aesthetics Studies
entries.text: Prajakta Balchandra Khanwalkar Assistant Professor Department of Design Industrial Design, Visual Ergonomics, Design Eductaion, Aesthetics Studies Prajakta Khanwalkar specializes in Architecture and Industrial Design. She holds a Bachelor Degree from PVP College of Architecture, Pune and Master's degree from Industrial Design Center, IIT Bombay. She has an experience of around two decades in the field of design and design education. Her professional work includes industrial design projects in consumer products, medical devices, commercial interiors, brand staretegies etc. She has been teaching in the realm of architecture and design. She has handled foundation classes, form studies, human centered design, new product development, inclusive product design and design projects. She is a core comittee member of the Association of Designers of India and is an active member in Pune Design Festival and Design Eductaion Festival happening in Pune. She is also a research scholar at IIT Guwahati.

entries.title: Pranati Prabhakar Waghodekar
entries.department: Department of Computer Engineering and Technology
entries.excerpt: A Red Hat–certified and Cloud-certified Architect (AWS & Azure), I bring over 7.5 years of academic experience and 2 years of industry exposure to deliver outcome-driven, industry-aligned education. My teaching philosophy emphasizes experiential learning, critical thinking, and hands-on skill development to prepare students for real-world challenges. I actively integrate emerging technologies such as Cloud, DevOps, and Blockchain into the curriculum, fostering innovation and research orientation among students. Currently pursuing a Ph.D. in Blockchain, I remain committed to advancing MIT-WPU’s vision of value-based, technology-driven education and holistic student development.
entries.text: Pranati Prabhakar Waghodekar Assistant Professor Department of Computer Engineering and Technology A Red Hat–certified and Cloud-certified Architect (AWS & Azure), I bring over 7.5 years of academic experience and 2 years of industry exposure to deliver outcome-driven, industry-aligned education. My teaching philosophy emphasizes experiential learning, critical thinking, and hands-on skill development to prepare students for real-world challenges. entries.title: Pravin Raghunath Minde
entries.excerpt: Construction Management, Ferrocement , Sustainable construction
entries.text: Pravin Raghunath Minde Assistant Professor Department of Civil Engineering Construction Management, Ferrocement , Sustainable construction Dr. Pravin Minde is working as an Assistant Professor and PG Coordinator (CEM) in the Department of Civil Engineering at MIT-WPU, Pune. He has over 12 years academic & administrative experience. He completed his Ph.D. in the domain of Construction Project Management from MIT-WPU in April 2025. He earned his M.E. in Construction Management in 2014 and B.E. in Civil Engineering in 2012, both from Pune University. Total Quality Management, Construction Management, Concrete Technology, Structural Auditing & retrofitting, Project Management, Architecture & Town planning, Building Design & Drawing https://doi.org/10.1016/j.matpr.2023.03.465 1. Minde, P., Patil, J. A comprehensive review of factors affecting 3D printing technology adaptation in the Indian construction sector. J Build Rehabil 10, 86 (2025). https://doi.org/10.1007/s41024-025-00604-w 2. Minde, P., Kulkarni, M. Revolutionizing Indian construction sector with sustainable modular LGSF-ferron composite construction technology. Innov. Infrastruct. Solut. 10, 25 (2025). https://doi.org/10.1007/s41062-024-01847-1 3. Kallimani, R., Minde, P. entries.title: Priya Bahirnath Kale
entries.excerpt: Education, Psychology
entries.text: Priya Bahirnath Kale Assistant Professor Department of Education Education, Psychology Mrs. Priya Kale is an experienced Assistant Professor at Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University, with 17 years of teaching experience. She holds a Master's degree in Marathi, a Master's in Education, a SET qualification, and a Diploma in Communication and Journalism. Currently, she is pursuing her Ph.D. in Stress Management and Happiness Index of secondary School students from Savitribai Phule Pune University.

entries.title: Prof. Akshay Motilal Baheti
entries.excerpt: Dr. Akshay M. Baheti is a Professor of Pharmacognosy at the School of Health Sciences and Technology, Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University, Pune, with over 25 years of experience in teaching, research, and administration in pharmaceutical education. He holds a B.Pharm from North Maharashtra University, M.Pharm in Pharmacognosy and Phytochemistry from Savitribai Phule Pune University, and a Ph.D. in Pharmaceutical Sciences from JNTU, Hyderabad, and is a Registered Patent Agent (IN/PA 5393). Dr. Baheti has served in key leadership roles as Head of the School of Pharmacy and Head, Department of Pharmacognosy. He has published over 30 peer-reviewed research papers, contributed to multiple book chapters, and has 14 patents filed/granted to his credit. He has guided several M.Pharm dissertations, successfully supervised one Ph.D. scholar to completion, and is currently supervising seven Ph.D. scholars in the areas of Pharmaceutical Sciences. Dr. Baheti has been recognized with several awards, including the MIT Foundation Day Award (2014) and the Best Innovative Teacher Award by Savitribai Phule Pune University (2017). He has also received a fellowship in Traditional Medicines and Integrative Health. Educational Qualifications M. Pharm, PhD

entries.title: Prof. Alamgir Sani
entries.excerpt: Dr. Alamgir Sani is actively pursuing and publishing research in the domain of Consumer Buying Behaviour and Brand Preference drivers; Multi-Sided Technology Platform Business Models; Passenger Cars Buying Behaviour and Preference Drivers, Impact of emerging technologies, including Blockchain Technologies on business models and Supply Chains, Application of AI and Human Mimicking Technology (HMT) in Customer Engagement etc.
entries.text: Prof. Alamgir Sani Associate Professor Department of Business Dr. Alamgir Sani is actively pursuing and publishing research in the domain of Consumer Buying Behaviour and Brand Preference drivers; Multi-Sided Technology Platform Business Models; Passenger Cars Buying Behaviour and Preference Drivers, Impact of emerging technologies, including Blockchain Technologies on business models and Supply Chains, Application of AI and Human Mimicking Technology (HMT) in Customer Engagement etc. Dr. Alamgir Sani is a distinguished academician and professional with over 24 years of combined experience across academia and industry, marked by a rich blend of teaching, research, and corporate exposure. He holds a Ph.D. in Management (Marketing) from Patna University and an MBA in Marketing and Finance from Aligarh Muslim University, and has qualified the UGC- NET. Dr. Sani’s corporate tenure includes impactful roles such as Cargo Manager with the Airports Authority of India and customer operations with EXL Services and DHL, which provided him deep expertise in logistics, air cargo operations, and global supply chains. Product and Brand Management, Integrated Marketing Communication, Business Research Methods, Social Media Marketing, Strategic Management, Services Marketing, Advertising and Media Planning, Marketing 4.0, Retail Management.

entries.title: Prof. Ameya Anil Patil
entries.excerpt: Finance, Management, International Business
entries.text: Prof. Ameya Anil Patil Associate Professor Department of Business Finance, Management, International Business Dr. Ameya Patil has more than 12 years of Teaching experience and 3.5 years of Industrial experience. He teaches subjects related to Finance and International Business. He has 25 Scopus indexed research articles to his credit and has authored more than 25 books. He has been invited as a keynote speaker at several national conferences. He is a member of the Indian Institute of Banking and Finance (IIBF) and ASCB(UK). 18 of his research works have appeared in Scopus and ABDC journals, and with reputed publishers such as Wiley, Inderscience, Taylor & Francis, IGI Global, Emerald and Springer.

Hiwale is a renowned professor with a Ph.D. in Electronics Engineering, specialized in Wireless Communication, from SGB Amravati University. With over 33 years of teaching experience, he is an expert in Wireless Communication, Signal Processing, Healthcare, and AI/ML. Dr. Hiwale has published numerous papers in reputed journals and conferences, and he has successfully supervised three Ph.D. scholars, with five more under his guidance currently. He is currently serving as the Associate Dean (Students Development) at the Faculty of Engineering & Technology, MITWPU, and has previously held positions such as Professor, Dean R & D, and Principal of MIT College of Engineering. Dr. Hiwale is a Fellow of IETE (The Institution of Electronics & Telecommunication Engineers) and IE (Institution of Engineers, India), and he is also the Chairman of The IET Pune Local Network. 1. entries.title: Prof. Anil Tukaram Pawar
entries.excerpt: Dr. Anil Pawar is an Associate Professor at the School of Health Sciences and Technology, Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University, Pune, with over 17 years of experience in academic teaching, research, and industry. He holds a B. Pharm from Savitribai Phule Pune University, an M. Pharm from Rajiv Gandhi University of Health Sciences, Karnataka, and a Ph.D. in Pharmacology. He has published more than 40 research articles and several review papers, authored books and book chapters, and holds five patents. He has guided M. Pharm students and is currently supervising Ph.D. scholars. Dr. Pawar has also received consultancy grants, reflecting his engagement in applied research and industry collaboration, and has established strong national and international research collaborations. He is a member of several prestigious professional associations, including the Association of Pharmaceutical Teachers of India (APTI), Indian Pharmaceutical Association (IPA), Indian Pharmacological Society (IPS), Society of Pharmaceutical Education and Research (SPER), International Association for the Study of Traditional Asian Medicine (IASTAM-India), and the Laboratory Animal Science Association of India (LASAI). He has received multiple awards for excellence in teaching and research, including the Ideal Teacher Award by Maharashtra Academy of Engineering and Educational Research (MAEER), Pune.

entries.excerpt: Power System Protection, Artificial Intelligence, Power Electronics
entries.text: Prof. Anjali Milind Purohit Associate Professor Department of Electrical and Electronics Engineering Power System Protection, Artificial Intelligence, Power Electronics Prof. Anjali Purohit is an accomplished academic and researcher in the field of Electrical Engineering. She completed her graduation in Electrical Engineering from Pune University in 1992 and later received her M.E. (Power Systems) from the same university in 2000. Currently, she is pursuing her PhD in power system protection from Amaravati University. Anjali has over 20 years of teaching experience and two years of industry experience. She is an Associate Professor in Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering at MIT-WPU. Prof. Purohit is a member of IE (I) and Life Member of ISTE. She has organized numerous STTP and conferences for the benefit of faculty and students. Prof. Purohit has also undertaken significant administrative responsibilities throughout her career. Her research interests include power system protection, power electronics, and artificial intelligence, among others. A. Purohit and V. entries.title: Prof. Anjoo Sharan Upadhyaya
entries.excerpt: Dr. Anjoo Sharan Upadhyaya is a distinguished Political Scientist from Banaras Hindu University (BHU), where she served over four decades in pivotal roles including Senior Professor and Chair of Political Science, Dean of Social Sciences, Director of the Centre for Nepal Studies, Director of the Centre for Integrated Rural Development, and Adjunct Professor at the Malaviya Centre for Peace Research. Her academic journey includes a PhD from BHU (1981), MA from Allahabad University (1974), post-doctoral research at the London School of Economics, Woodrow Wilson International Centre for Scholars, Brown University, and an Advanced International Diploma in Conflict Resolution from Uppsala University. She has held prestigious positions such as Research Director at the UN University Institute on Conflict Resolution and Ethnicity (UK), Scholar-in-Residence at Woodrow Wilson Centre (USA), foundational ICCR Chair at Tribhuvan University (Nepal), India Chair by ICSSR New Delhi, and visiting professorships at institutions like Wellesley College, Concordia University, Stanford University, PRIO Oslo, and universities across Sweden, Germany, Australia, Canada, and Poland.

Her academic journey includes a PhD from BHU (1981), MA from Allahabad University (1974), post-doctoral research at the London School of Economics, Woodrow Wilson International Centre for Scholars, Brown University, and an Advanced International Diploma in Conflict Resolution from Uppsala University. She has held prestigious positions such as Research Director at the UN University Institute on Conflict Resolution and Ethnicity (UK), Scholar-in-Residence at Woodrow Wilson Centre (USA), foundational ICCR Chair at Tribhuvan University (Nepal), India Chair by ICSSR New Delhi, and visiting professorships at institutions like Wellesley College, Concordia University, Stanford University, PRIO Oslo, and universities across Sweden, Germany, Australia, Canada, and Poland.

entries.title: Prof. Anuradha Shantanu Kanade
entries.department: Department of Computer Science and Applications
entries.excerpt: Dr. Anuradha Kanade is the Program Director of the Department of Computer Science and Applications at Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University, Pune. She holds a Ph.D. from Savitribai Phule Pune University, an M.Phil., MCA, MBA (Business Analytics), and a B.Sc. (Physics). With over 24 years of academic and industry experience, she has made significant contributions to teaching, research, academic leadership, and curriculum development. She is starving for the excellence to ensure the overall growth of the students, department and faculty members. Her teaching philosophy emphasizes learner-centric, value-based, and outcome-oriented education that nurtures technical competence, ethical values, innovation, and lifelong learning. She is passionate about motivating students through research, industry interaction, internships, and project-based learning Through her contributions in research, patents, publications, academic leadership, and industry collaborations, she actively supports MIT-WPU’s vision of promoting excellence in education, innovation, research, and holistic development of socially responsible global professionals. She is an approved Ph.D. supervisor and has guided research scholars in emerging areas of computer science. Dr. Kanade has authored 50+ research publications, several books, and holds multiple Indian and UK patents, with a number of them granted. She has successfully completed funded research projects and actively collaborates with academia and industry to promote innovation and applied research.

Chaudhari has pursued the BE in Industrial Electronics from Amravati University in 1989 and then METelE and PhD in Engineering from Jadavpur University, Kolkata, India, in 1993 and 2000, respectively. After being a Full Professor in Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering with the Pune Institute of Computer Technology and the Dean and Principal of the International Institute of Information Technology, Pune, he joined MIT World Peace University (then MIT Pune) in 2014 as a Professor. He held additional responsibilities as Dean of Academics (July 2021 - Sept 2022), Associate Dean - Faculty of Engineering and Technology (Feb 2020 - June 2023), and Head of the School of Electrical/Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering (July 2017 - December 2023) at MIT World Peace University. 1 . He holds a Bachelor's, Master's, and PhD in Chemical Engineering, and has amassed 17 years of teaching and research experience. Dr. Bhutada is a prolific writer and presenter, having published more than 20 research papers in peer-reviewed journals and conferences, and holds 5 Indian Patents in Environmental Engineering. He is currently leading a RGSTC Funded research project and has completed 2 BCUD Funded research projects from University of Pune. He is also a core team member of MIT World Peace University's Admission Committee for UG and PG Admissions, and is instrumental in the development of the laboratory and curriculum. Additionally, he frequently interacts with industry leaders as placement coordinator and facilitates industry-institute collaborations. entries.title: Prof. Ganesh Shivaji Ingle
entries.excerpt: Prf. Dr. Ganesh Ingle is an Associate Professor in Department of Civil Engineering at Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University, Pune. He has completed his Ph.D. in Transportation Engineering from College of Engineering Pune (COEP) in the year 2018, received his B. E. in Civil Engineering from Govt. College of Engineering, Amravati in the year 2000 and Masters (M. E. Civil) in technical Engineering in the year of 2003, from College of Engineering Pune (COEP). He is working with MIT-WPU since August 2008 and has been teaching various subjects like soil Mechanics, Foundation Engineering, soil structure and interaction, concrete technology, construction materials and advanced transportation Engineering. As a part of Ph. D work, he has indigenously designed and developed a Laboratory Full scale Accelerated Pavement Testing Set Up (APT) in COEP campus through an R&D Grant of 13.5 Lac. This is a unique facility which is not available in India where the performance of any pavement can be tested within a short period. It was also applied for Indian Patent. He has published more than 15 research papers in Scopus and SCI Journals and also published three Indian Patent and one Australian Patent granted on stabilization of soil. He is also doing Testing and consultancy in the field of Geotechnical Engineering and completed more than 4.0 Lac testing and consultancy related to slope stability analysis in Chiplun, Dapoli area as well as geotechnical investigation for SBC of soil for some of the project. He also conducted the training sessions for Border Road Organization (BRO) Engineers related to Geotechnical aspects during road constructions. He also received the BCUD Grants of 1.40 Lac.

entries.title: Prof. Gauri Bilaye
entries.department: Department of Computer Engineering and Technology
entries.excerpt: My philosophy as a teacher will be based on stimulating curiosity, critical thinking, and problem solving in the real world. My goal is to build a non-discriminatory, interactive learning classroom, in which students form an active group, challenge their thoughts and possibilities, and relate theory with practice. As a teacher, my driving power is to guide students towards achieving not only academic results by also teaching them how to achieve ethical values, creativity and life-long learning. In line with the vision of the holistic and value-based education at MIT-WPU, I would be able to make a contribution through creative teaching methods, interdisciplinary cooperation, and the learning process based on research that would help students become responsible professional beings and influential global citizens.

entries.title: Prof. Gautam Surendra Bapat
entries.excerpt: Dr. Gautam Bapat is Associate Dean – Faculty Affairs and Associate Professor of Marketing at the School of Business, MIT World Peace University, Pune. With over a decade of experience, he has held key academic leadership roles, contributing to program design, faculty development, and institutional advancement. His academic philosophy integrates industry relevance with scholarly rigor. Dr. Bapat holds a Ph.D. in Management, focusing on digital marketing for student recruitment, along with qualifications in marketing, computer applications, and zoology. His interdisciplinary approach informs his teaching in branding, digital transformation, and marketing strategy. A prolific researcher, he has published over 38 papers, presented at international forums, authored textbooks, and holds five patents in areas like IoT and social service systems. His work explores AI in marketing, higher education strategies, and consumer behavior. Recognized with awards from IIM Ahmedabad and IRSSM, he is a sought-after speaker and an advocate for innovation in management education.

entries.title: Prof. Girish Sharad Barpande
entries.excerpt: Dr. Girish Sharad Barpande is a multi-faceted professional with diverse interests ranging from aerospace and design to social issues and films. With a Ph.D. in Mechanical Engineering (Aerospace-Scramjet) from Savitribai Phule Pune University, he has 27 years of professional experience, out of which 24 years are in teaching. His areas of interest and expertise include engineering design, design thinking, computational fluid dynamics, tribology, and total quality management. He has authored 11 research papers in various international journals and conferences. He founded and headed the SUPRA SAEINDIA team at MIT since 2011 with an impressive track record at national and international levels.

entries.title: Prof. Hossein Martin Fazeli
entries.excerpt: Hossein Martin Fazeli is a distinguished Iranian writer, producer, and director who has been an A-list filmmaker in North America for many years and currently serves as a Professor of Practice at Dadasaheb Phalke International Film School. With over two decades of experience in producing, directing, and writing fiction and non-fiction television and films, Fazeli has built an illustrious career marked by his extensive international collaborations across more than a dozen countries. His works have been broadcast globally on leading networks such as CBC, BBC, ARTE, and Canal+, earning 37 international awards and critical acclaim.

entries.title: Prof. Kundlik Vitthal Mali
entries.excerpt: Dr. Kundlik Mali is a Professor and AHoS in the field of Mechanical Engineering. He earned his Ph.D. in Mechanical Engineering from SPPU Pune in 2014 and completed his M.Tech. from COEP Pune. His expertise lies in the field of Thermal Sciences - HVACR, technology, and he has worked with leading companies such as Godrej and Daikin AC Ltd. to improve the energy efficiency of air conditioners. Dr. Mali has organized several workshops and training programs for students and faculty members in the area of Thermal Engineering. In the last three years, he has taught courses such as RAC, Heat transfer, Fluid Machinery, Numerical Methods, and CFD.

entries.title: Prof. Manasi Mishra
entries.excerpt: Plant defence proteins and peptides, bioactive molecules, protein engineering, botanical therapeutics, molecular diversity, and evolution.
entries.text: Prof. Manasi Mishra Associate Professor Department of Biosciences and Technology Plant defence proteins and peptides, bioactive molecules, protein engineering, botanical therapeutics, molecular diversity, and evolution. Dr. Manasi Mishra is working as Associate Professor in Department of Biosciences & Technology, MIT-World Peace University. She has been actively working in the area of plant immunity proteins and peptides involved in defense against insect pest infestation and those with relevance in the context of crop improvement. Her research has been focused on structure-function correlation of diverse plant defense proteins, molecular diversity and their functional significance. She is also working extensively towards understanding the defense peptides distribution within individual plants and their tissues, factors influencing their expression, gene architecture, post-translational mechanisms, bioactivities and designing synthetic peptides directed towards therapeutic molecular targets. Biology for engineers, Synthetic Biology and Biologics production; Genomics and Genetic engineering, Translational approaches in Biotechnology Mishra M, Singh V, Tellis MB, Joshi RS, Singh S (2021) Repurposing the McoTI-II rigid molecular scaffold in to inhibitor of ‘papain superfamily’ cysteine proteases. entries.title: Prof. Mangesh Vilas Bedekar
entries.department: Department of Computer Engineering and Technology
entries.excerpt: Dr. Mangesh V. Bedekar earned his Ph.D in Computer Science and Engineering from BITS Pilani, India, after completing his graduation from SSGMCE, Shegaon, and obtaining his Masters from BITS Pilani. He currently serves as the Professor & Dean, School of Computer Science & Engineering at MIT-WPU. Dr. Bedekar's research interests are focused on areas such as Web Data Mining, Web Personalization, User Interface Design, User Interface Improvements, Browser Customization, Affective Computing, and Information Visualization. Throughout his career, he has supervised numerous bachelors and masters students and has many national and international publications to his credit. In addition to his academic pursuits, Dr. Bedekar is an outdoors enthusiast and a certified First-Aider who enjoys hiking and trekking regularly. entries.title: Prof. Manish Kumar Sharma
entries.excerpt: Prof. Manish has around 5.5 years of working experience in Industry and academia. His PhD is from the Indian Institute of Technology, Kharagpur (QS World-Ranked 215, 2026). He has earlier worked with the Centre for Digital Transformation at the Indian Institute of Management, Ahmedabad (QS World-ranked 27th in Management, 2026), where he contributed to a research project on Responsible AI and FinTech analysis. He has qualified UGC-NET in Management. He has done his post-graduation with MBA in Finance (University Gold Medallist) and B. Tech. in Electronics and Communication Engineering. His research interests lie in the areas of International Business and Economics, International Trade, Digital Economy, FinTech, Digital Transformation, Artificial Intelligence, Econometrics, Data Science, and Machine Learning. He has gained industry exposure for around four years, working both in India and internationally in East Africa. He earlier worked with the Department of Economic Affairs, Ministry of Finance, Govt. of India, on a research project titled “Digital Economy and Fintech in India: Opportunities and Challenges” as a PhD research intern. He is a published author with three published articles in reputed journals and has many more in the pipeline. He is also a peer reviewer at Electronic Commerce Research, Springer Nature and International Journal of Finance and Economics (IJFE), Wiley Publications. He has travelled to more than ten countries around the world during his research work, including the Netherlands, Spain, Belgium, France, Germany, the UK, Ethiopia, Morocco, Turkey, the UAE, and Qatar and has presented his research papers at many international conferences in India, Spain, the UK, and Morocco. He received two best research paper awards, one at IBRS 2024, Morocco and the other one at BIT MESRA in India. He aims to make an impactful contribution to the world through his quality research work. He is efficient working with diverse Computing and Statistical tools like Stata, Python, Tableau, Power BI, Jupyter Labs, Google Colab, SPSS, SAP ERP, Oracle Citrix ERP, and Tally ERP. He is a member of the International Trade and Finance Association, the Spanish Economic Association, the Econometric Society - Yale University, USA, and the Indian Society of Labour Economics.

entries.title: Prof. Narendra Laxman Mathakari
entries.excerpt: Radiation processing of polymers and composites; viscoelastic and acoustic properties of liquid mixtures.
entries.text: Prof. Narendra Laxman Mathakari Professor of Practice Department of Physics Radiation processing of polymers and composites; viscoelastic and acoustic properties of liquid mixtures. Dr. Narendra Mathakari obtained his M.Sc. (Physics) degree from Department of Physics, SPPU in 1989 and Ph.D degree from the same department and university in 2009, He has a vast teaching experience of over 30 years and research experience of over 15 years. He has published 11 research papers in international journals. and 4 papers in international conference proceedings. He has also presented papers at 9 national and international conferences. He has also published 10 science popularization articles in newspapers and delivered science popularization talks at various places. His research interests include radiation processing of polymers and composites and viscoelastic and acoustic properties of binary and ternary liquids. He currently has research facilities, including an Abbe refractometer, a density measurement device, a viscometry system, and an ultrasonic interferometer all with constant temperature baths. He has also published a book on “Experiments in Engineering Physics”. Currently his book on “Concepts of Engineering Physics” is in the process of publication. During his career in MIT WPU, he received the MIT Foundation Day Award in 2008 and MIT Ideal Teacher Award in 2009.

entries.text: Prof. Netra Manoj Lokhande Associate Professor Department of Electrical and Electronics Engineering Image Processing, Power Systems, IoT, Robotics Dr. Netra Lokhande is an Associate Professor in the School of School of Engineering and Technology at MIT World Peace University, Pune. She holds a PhD in Electronics, a Master's degree in Electrical Engineering, and a Bachelor's degree in Electrical and Electronics Engineering. With 25 years of experience in teaching, Dr. Lokhande has expertise in Image Processing, Power Systems, Robotics, and Power Electronics. She has published 30 research papers and is a member of various professional bodies such as ISTE, IEI, IAENG, and IAEME. 1) A MATLAB based GUI for Multiframe Super Resolution Reconstruction of different gray scale images with and without adaptive filter. 2)Multi-frame Super Resolution Reconstruction and Image Quality Assessment of Different Gray Scale Images. 3)Robust Multiframe Superresolution Reconstruction of different gray scale images for real time data with and without adaptive filter. 4)A Comprehensive Study of Different Image Super-Resolution Reconstruction Algorithms. 5)Real Time Static Gesture Recognition using Time of Flight Camera. 6)A Machine Learning Access for Person Identification using Dental X-Ray Images."

entries.title: Prof. Nivedita Gunesh Gogate
entries.excerpt: Dr. Nivedita Gogate is currently working as an Associate Professor in the Department of Civil Engineering, Dr Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University, Pune from 2018. She joined the MIT-WPU in 1996 and since then she has been associated with teaching, administrative, research and examination work of the university. She completed her BE (Civil Engineering) from COEP followed by M.Env.Eng.Sc. (MS in Environmental Engineering) from University of New South Wales (UNSW), Sydney, Australia. She developed training manuals for Environmental Awareness in Industry for managers and workers in association with an Australian consulting firm. She has an industrial experience of 2 years and teaching experience of 26 years. She completed her PhD in Civil-Environmental Engineering from COEP. She has published 5 research papers in international journals of good repute and presented papers in international conferences. She, along with her colleagues has published an Indian patent. Presently, she is working as a PhD Coordinator of Department of Civil Engineering.

entries.text: Prof. Pankaj Nivrutti Dhatrak Associate Professor Department of Mechanical Engineering Bio-mechanical Engineering, Medical Devices, Vibration and Fatigue, FEA Dr. Pankaj Dhatrak is an accomplished Assistant Professor in the School of Mechanical Engineering at MIT-World Peace University, Pune, India. With a PhD in Biomechanical Engineering from Savitribai Phule Pune University, he has a strong background in Finite Element Analysis of Automotive components, Biomechanics, Biomaterials, and their applications. He has over 5 years of industrial experience in vibration and fatigue analysis of various automotive components using different CAE tools, along with more than 15 years of academic experience teaching UG/PG students. Dr. Dhatrak is an author of over 45+ peer-reviewed research articles and book chapters in reputed Journal and Conference Proceedings. Recently, he has been actively involved in designing and developing biomedical devices. He has served as a Technical Advisory Committee Member for 5+ International conferences and has reviewed manuscripts for over 5+ reputable peer-reviewed journals. entries.title: Prof. Pradeep Kumar Tiwari
entries.department: Department of Computer Science and Applications
entries.excerpt: Dr. Pradeep Kumar Tiwari is an Associate Professor at Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University. He has previously worked at Birla Global University and Manipal University Jaipur as an Associate Professor and Assistant Professor. Dr. Tiwari holds a Ph.D. in the Faculty of Engineering, along with M.Tech in CSE and M.Phil in Computer Science. Currently he is pursuing a Postdoctoral. His research areas cloud computing, distributed systems, AI, and IoT. With over fifteen years of experience in teaching and research, Dr. Tiwari has authored more than fifty publications in Web of Science and Scopus indexed journals, conferences, and book chapters in the field of computer science. Additionally, he has received three patent grants and published three patents. One scholar has completed and five scholars are currently pursuing their Ph.D. under the supervision of Dr. Tiwari.

entries.title: Prof. Prakash Mohan Mainkar
entries.department: Department of Electrical and Electronics Engineering
entries.excerpt: Dr. Prakash Mohan Mainkar obtained his Bachelor's degree in Electronics Engineering from Shivaji University in 2001, followed by a Master's degree in Electronics (VLSI Design) in 2007. He later completed his Doctor of Philosophy (Engineering) from RTM Nagpur University, Nagpur, India, in 2019. Currently, he serves as the Director of Admissions and an Associate Professor in Electronics & Communication Engineering at MIT-WPU. Along with his teaching responsibilities, he actively participates in various institute committees and contributes to the development of laboratory infrastructure and curriculum. Dr. Mainkar regularly engages with industry professionals to explore employment opportunities for students and facilitate international university collaborations for MIT-WPU. He has undertaken several international study tours to universities in China, Singapore, Hong Kong, and Nepal, in his capacity as an International Study Tour Coordinator. Over the past 8 years, he has also served as a National Study Tour Coordinator and organized visits to numerous industries throughout India to provide students with exposure to real-world applications of their coursework.

entries.title: Prof. Pranali Kashinath Kosamkar
entries.department: Department of Computer Engineering and Technology
entries.excerpt: Dr. Pranali Kosamkar is an Associate Professor in the Department of Computer Engineering and Technology at Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University, Pune. Prof. Pranali is well known for her fundamental and impactful contribution to the field of computing education, elevating every student she has mentored through a consistent, selfless teaching service for more than 20 years. Her research interests are in Machine Learning, Deep Learning, Information Retrieval, and Quantum Computing. Prof. Pranali is also a well-respected technologist. She has delivered sessions on Big Data Analytics with Python to selected officers of the Indian Armed Forces at Military Institute of Training, Girinagar Pune. She has guided many undergraduate and graduate projects and has national and international paper publications to her credit. Her innovative teaching of basic courses like compiler design, database management system, data mining and principles of programming languages has built a high recognition among the student community in the institute MIT-WPU, Pune.

entries.title: Prof. Prashant Joshi
entries.excerpt: Dr. Prashant Joshi is an accomplished researcher and academician in the field of medicinal chemistry, specializing in natural product-based drug discovery. He earned his B. Pharm. from Mahakal Institute of Pharmaceutical Sciences, Ujjain (India) in 2006, followed by an M. Pharm. from the School of Pharmaceutical Sciences, RGPV Bhopal (India) in 2009. In 2018, he obtained his Ph.D. under the supervision of Dr. Sandip B. Bharate at CSIR-Indian Institute of Integrative Medicine, Jammu (India), through the Academy of Scientific and Innovative Research (AcSIR), New Delhi, as a CSIR-Senior Research Fellow. Dr. Joshi has held academic positions in pharmaceutical and natural product chemistry departments at SPTM, NMIMS Shirpur. Currently, he serves as an Associate Professor at the School of Health Science and Technology, Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University, Pune, India. His research interests focus on medicinal chemistry and natural products, aiming to discover new therapeutics for multi-drug-resistant (MDR) cancers and bacterial infections. He has an impressive record of 32 research publications and 9 US patents granted in the field of natural source-based drug discovery.

entries.title: Prof. Rajashri Amit Kadam
entries.excerpt: Dr. Rajashri Kadam is dedicated Associate Professor in the domain of Marketing and currently handling additional responsibility as the Associate Dean – Academics at the School of Business since January 2022. With over 22 years of combined industry and academic experience, she has been a passionate educator for the past 18 years, known for her interactive and learner- centric pedagogy. Dr. Kadam holds a Ph.D. in Marketing Management and an Executive Diploma in Digital Marketing from IIM Jammu. Her teaching interests include Advertising, Services Marketing, Integrated Marketing Communication, and Strategic Marketing. She has contributed significantly to academic research with publications in reputed national and international journals, Scopus-indexed book chapters, Case studies and numerous conference papers. She has also guided student-led case studies and research publications. She is also a Ph.D. supervisor and certified trainer, with prior experience in consulting and content development. Known for her strong rapport with students and alumni, Dr. Kadam continues to inspire learners with her dynamic teaching, academic leadership, and commitment to educational excellence.

entries.title: Prof. Rajib Kumar Sinharay
entries.excerpt: A geophysicist with total 25 years of experiences in industry as well as research mainly in geophysical investigations, hydrocarbon exploration, engineering geophysics, deep crustal studies and Antarctic research. Having a MScTech and PhD degree in Applied Geophysics from IIT(ISM), Dhanbad, I have worked for Central Water and Power Research Station (CWPRS), Govt. of India, WesternGeco- Schlumberger (SLB) and Reliance Industries Limited (RIL). Presently working as a Professor in School of Petroleum Engineering, Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University (MITWPU), Pune. My fields of researches are Petroleum Exploration and Production, Unconventional Hydrocarbon, Geothermal Exploration, Underground Carbon Sequestration (UCS), Machine Learning (ML), Mineral Exploration, Antarctic Geoscience and near surface geophysics. A passionate innovator, vigorous learner, motivated leader and critical thinker. Published more than 25 research paper in different peer-reviewed journals, books and conferences.

entries.title: Prof. Samarth Dilip Patwardhan
entries.excerpt: Samarth D. Patwardhan serves as Head of Centre for Subsea Engineering Research (CSER), and Professor in the Department of Petroleum Engineering at MITWPU, Pune, India. His research interests include production and reservoir engineering, enhanced oil recovery, HSE, as well as applying data analytics to the oil and gas domain. Through research grants he has procured; he has been instrumental in revamping the reservoir characterization laboratory and upgrading the simulation lab with industry-benchmarked software tools at the department. He is involved in collaborating with industry to further the academic and research partnerships for the recently commissioned CSER lab, a one-of-its-kind lab established through a common shared vision with Aker Solutions. Further, his current efforts are also directed towards setting up a stateof-the-art research lab for testing various characteristics of Nano-dusts, which span across industries. The lab is jointly funded by the Department of Science and Technology (DST), Government of India (GoI), and Techniche Engineering Private Limited (TEPL), an HSE company based out of Pune, India. He works closely with his undergraduate, graduate and doctorate students on topics spanning from characterizing various rock types using data analytics, to enhanced oil recovery, and applying computational fluid dynamics to problems being faced in the oil and gas industry. He serves as a reviewer on multiple prestigious peer-reviewed journals and funding agencies and is involved in collaborative work on research proposals on multiple platforms, including academia and industry, both at national and international levels. He is the recipient of the 2019 APJ Abdul Kalam MIT WPU Research and Innovation Award, and the SPE 2022 Regional Distinguished Achievement Award for Petroleum Engineering Faculty for South Asia and Pacific Region. Samarth serves as a Member on the Enhanced Recovery Committee (ERC), formed under the Ministry of Petroleum and Natural Gas (MoPNG), Government of India (GoI), Director on the SPE International Mumbai Section board, Faculty Advisor to the MITWPU – SPE Student Chapter, and a Member on the Energy4Me committee formed for South Asia, under SPE International. Apart from serving as a SPE Student Chapter Faculty Advisor since 2014, he has volunteered for multiple activities within SPE, including acting in the capacity of a judge for Regional Student Paper Contest (RSPC) for all categories (U.G., P.G., and Ph.D.) since 2017, as well as volunteering under SPE Cares initiative. Through industry support, he has been instrumental in organizing the RSPC at the MITWPU campus in 2014, as well as planning and organizing multiple technical workshops, guest lectures and field visits for student members, keeping up with SPE’s mission of disseminating and exchanging knowledge concerning oil and gas industry, to build technical and professional competence in the next generation workforce, thereby bridging the gap between industry and academics.

entries.excerpt: Politics of the Middle EAST, International Relations, Political Theory, Indian Foreign Policy
entries.text: Prof. Samuel Jacob Kuruvilla Associate Professor Department of Liberal Arts Politics of the Middle EAST, International Relations, Political Theory, Indian Foreign Policy Dr. Samuel Jacob Kuruvilla is an Associate Professor at the MIT-WPU School of Liberal Arts. He completed his BA, majoring in Indian History (with minors in Economics & Political Science) from CMS College, Kottayam, followed by an MA in Politics & International Relations from the School of International Relations & Politics, Mahatma Gandhi University, Kottayam. He pursued his M.Phil in Diplomacy at Jawaharlal Nehru University (JNU), New Delhi, and obtained his Ph.D. in Religion & Politics from the University of Exeter, United Kingdom. Previously, he served as a Post-Doctoral Fellow at the School of International Relations and Politics of Mahatma Gandhi University, Kottayam, Kerala. Additionally, he received his Doctorate in Politics and Theology from the University of Exeter in the United Kingdom.

entries.text: Prof. Shailendra Kumar Kale Professor Department of Business Logistics, Supply Chain Management, Operations, Entrepreneurship Dr. Shailendra Kumar Kale is a proficient teacher with over 23 years of experience in the faculty of management. He is passionate about work. Being an academic leader, he has proficiency in handling the institutional academic activities and administrative activities as well. As a Ph.D. research guide, he worked to develop a research attitude among his scholars. Four research scholars completed Ph.D. under his guidance. He has worked for developing the curriculum for MBA in academic autonomy structure. Dr. Kale has served as a member of the Board of Studies and Academic Council at a few autonomous colleges and universities. In his research domain, he has published 2 books, 2 book chapters and his 13 research articles are indexed in Scopus database. He has completed two minor research projects. 1. Dr. Shailendra Kumar Kale (2020), “Milk Run Technique to ensure effective Just in Time Technique practices”, International Journal of Advanced Science and Technology Vol. 29, No. 8s, (2020), ISSN- 2005-4238 pp. 1302-1307, 2. entries.title: Prof. Shamim Mohammad
entries.excerpt: Dr. Shamim received his first degree in rehabilitation sciences from the National institute of Empowerment of People with Intellectual Disabilities, Secunderbad; MA and M.Phil. degrees from the Indian Universities. He majored in Public Health from Ohio University under International Ford Foundation Fellowship program in 2008; his doctoral work from the Symbiosis International University, Pune in Health Sciences in 2018. He taught 7 years at Umm AL Qura University, Makkah, KSA, 1 year at OP Jindal Global University, Sonipat, Haryana, and presently teaching at MIT WPU, Pune. He has also practiced public health through various agencies government and non-government agencies for the last past 15 years. He has more than a dozen publications in his credit. Furthermore,, he is a licensed from the RCI as a rehab practitioner.

entries.title: Prof. Shilpa Samir Chapadgaonkar
entries.excerpt: Bioprocess and enzyme technology, biopharmaceutical production and purification, tissue engineering, disease models, and sustainable biotechnology.
entries.text: Prof. Shilpa Samir Chapadgaonkar Associate Professor and Program Director Department of Biosciences and Technology Bioprocess and enzyme technology, biopharmaceutical production and purification, tissue engineering, disease models, and sustainable biotechnology. Dr. Shilpa S. Chapadgaonkar is an experienced academician and researcher with expertise in upstream processing, bioseparation, and enzyme technology. With over 15 years of teaching experience at UG, PG, and PhD levels, she has guided four PhD candidates to completion and is currently supervising three more, along with several M.Sc. and M.Tech. projects. She is leading the Department of Biosciences and Technology at MITWPU as Program Director. She has held key roles such as MSc (Biotech) Program Coordinator, Research Cluster Head, and PhD Program Coordinator, contributing significantly to NAAC and UGC audits. Her research focuses on drug discovery, bioprocess development for biopharmaceuticals, and investigating genetic disorders like infertility and autism. Passionate about sustainability, she has developed technologies for waste valorization, enzyme formulations, and textile wastewater treatment. Dr. Chapadgaonkar’s work has been supported by grants from the Science and Engineering Research Board (SERB), and she has led industrial collaborations with global companies like BASF and Gennova Biopharmaceuticals. Bioprocess

entries.title: Prof. Shital Pathak
entries.excerpt: Dr. Shitak Pathak is a seasoned pharmaceutical professional with over 28 years of illustrious career in industry. Dr. Shital has expertise in Analytical Research, in vitro bio equivalence, quality and compliance. Currently he is working as senior vice president & head of Analytical Research and in vitro bio study lab at Glenmark Pharmaceutical Ltd. He has worked with major Pharma companies like Ranbaxy, Wockhardt, Sandoz and Apotex. His industry experience is across the globe e.g. US, Canada, Europe, and Latin America. He has experience in all dosage forms like oral solids, liquids, topical dosages, injections, and respiratory products. Has an in depth understanding of regulatory science and different markets.

entries.title: Prof. Shivprakash Bhagwatrao Barve
entries.excerpt: Prof. Dr. Shivprakash B. Barve is a Mechanical Engineering Ph.D. holder from Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj University, Kolhapur. He completed his post-graduation in Mechanical Engineering from the Government College of Engineering Karad and graduation in Mechanical Engineering from Jawaharlal Nehru Engineering College Aurangabad with distinction. With areas of specialization in Materials, Composites, Nanocomposites, Manufacturing System, Renewable Energy Technologies, Power Generation, Waste Heat Recovery, Emission, Operations Research, Research Methodology, Applied Thermodynamics, Project Management, and Operations Research, he possesses exceptional skills and expertise. He has been honored with the MIT Ideal Teacher Award in September 2017 and elected as a Member of the Committee of the Institution of Engineers (India), Pune Local Center for the period of August 2021-July 2023. Dr. Barve has also served as a Member of the Board of Studies (BOS) for Mechanical Engineering at Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University, Pune (2019-2021) and recognized as a PG Teacher and Ph.D. Guide in Mechanical Engineering by the same institution in 2017

entries.excerpt: Dr. Shridevi Karande has been working as an assistant professor at Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University since 2005. She holds a PhD in the AI and ML healthcare domain, and her research project, titled "Detection of Pre-Dementia," received funding from IBM. Her research interests include Artificial Intelligence (AI), Machine Learning (ML), Big Data Analytics, Cloud Computing, and Healthcare Analytics.

entries.title: Prof. Sushil Kumar Gupta
entries.excerpt: Dr. Sushil Kumar Gupta brings over 21 years of distinguished experience, equally divided between corporate leadership and academic excellence. A UGC-NET qualified professional in Management (June 2012), he holds a Ph.D. in Management from Amity University, Uttar Pradesh, and is an alumnus of IIM Ahmedabad, having completed the prestigious 43rd Faculty Development Programme in Pedagogy and Research Methods (April–May 2023). His academic portfolio includes 12 Scopus-indexed publications and 9 ABDC-listed papers (2 in category B and 7 in category C), alongside five patents in the domains of finance and behavioural analytics. He is also a recipient of a research grant from IIM Ahmedabad, further reinforcing his scholarly engagement. His research focuses on contemporary trends in finance, fintech, wealth management etc. He has been honoured with the Outstanding Educator Award 2025 by Lexicon Group of Institutions and the Global Teacher Award 2021 by AKS Education.

entries.text: Prof. Uma Ramachandra Pujeri Associate Professor Department of Computer Engineering and Technology Computer Networks, Cryptography, loud computing, encryption algorithm, Post Quantum Lattice algorithm Dr. Uma R Pujeri was born in Sangli, India in 1981. She earned her M.Tech degree from PSG Tech College of Engineering, Coimbatore in 2008 and received her doctorate degree from Anna University, Chennai in May 2017. Her research area focuses on computer network congestion control algorithms. With fourteen years of teaching experience, she has worked as an Assistant Professor at Adithya College of Engineering, Coimbatore for six years and is currently serving as an Associate Professor at Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University, Pune, Maharashtra. Dr. Pujeri is a Life Member of the Indian Society for Technical Education (ISTE) and has conducted several workshops, FDPs, and conferences. Her post-doctoral fellowship from Srinivas University, Mangalore, under the guidance of Dr. P.S. Aitthal, is registered under the ID 20SUPDROO9. Dr. Pujeri has published 40 research papers in international journals, and her PhD thesis is available on the UGC website https://shodhganga.inflibnet.ac.in/handle/10603/196148. Contact her at uma.pujeri@mitwpu.edu.in. Research Papers 1 :“A Modified Congestion Control Algorithm for TCP”, International Journal of Applied Engineering Research ISSN 0973-4

entries.title: Prof. Vijay Kumar Kalyani
entries.excerpt: Neural networks, physics-informed neural networks, mathematical modelling, and mathematical seismology.
entries.text: Prof. Vijay Kumar Kalyani Professor of Practice Department of Mathematics and Statistics Neural networks, physics-informed neural networks, mathematical modelling, and mathematical seismology. Dr. Vijay Kumar Kalyani is currently serving as Professor of Practice at Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University, Pune. He is a distinguished mathematician and computational scientist with over 35 years of experience in teaching and research. He obtained his Ph.D. in Applied Mathematics from the Indian School of Mines (IIT), Dhanbad, in 1988. He also earned an M.Phil. with a Gold Medal and an M.Tech. by research in Computer Applications. His research focuses on Artificial Neural Networks, Physics-Informed Neural Networks, mathematical modelling, and seismic wave propagation. He has served in senior scientific positions at CSIR-CIMFR, Dhanbad, including Chief Scientist (Scientist-G). He has supervised Ph.D. and postgraduate theses and taught B.Tech. and M.Tech. students. He has published more than 25 research papers in SCI, Scopus, and international journals. His work includes numerical simulation, deep learning, and computational geophysics. He continues to contribute actively to advanced research and education in applied mathematics and neural networks.

entries.title: Prof. Vijaykumar Bilolikar
entries.excerpt: Prof Bilolikar has a rich academic and administrative experience of 28 years and is a professor of Operations Management at MIT-WPU. He has handled various administrative responsibilities such as Dean of Student Affairs, Controller of examination, NBA coordinator etc. He holds a Bachelors in Production Engineering from Government College of Engineering and a Masters in manufacturing engineering. He holds a doctoral degree from Indian Institute of Technology Bombay in the area of operations management. His papers have been published in reputed international conferences and journals including International Journal of Production Research, Benchmarking an International Journal, International Journal of Logistics and Management, Annals of operations research etc. He is on the reviewer board of various journals. His research interests include supply chain management, application of AI techniques to operations and project management, simulation of manufacturing systems, sustainable manufacturing and Industry 4.0 architecture.

entries.title: Prof. Vikram Shete
entries.excerpt: Dr. Vikram Shete is an accomplished entrepreneur, mentor, and educator with over 15 years of experience fostering innovation and building entrepreneurial ecosystems. As an Associate Professor of Entrepreneurship at MIT World Peace University and a mentor with premier institutions like Stanford Seed, Atal Incubation Centre, and BITS Pilani, he has empowered hundreds of aspiring founders. His flagship course, Entrepreneurship Blueprint, has enabled students to launch viable startups, some of which have secured incubation offers. Driven by a mission to nurture the next generation of entrepreneurs, Dr. Shete blends academic rigor with real-world startup insights to deliver transformative learning experiences.

entries.title: Rahul Gaikwad
entries.excerpt: Rahul Gaikwad is an illustrator and design tutor with over 7 years of combined experience in the education and creative industries. He holds a Bachelor's degree in Fine Art from Sir J.J. School of Art and a Master's in Illustration and Animation from Coventry University, UK. His professional work spans across diverse domains such as illustration, asset building, animation, character design, branding, graphic design and storyboarding. Showcasing his versatility and command over visual storytelling. Rahul's teaching approach blends industry insight with academic rigor, helping students build strong creative foundations. In 2012, he was honored as a young cartoonist by the legendary R.K. Laxman through a recognition by the Times of India, marking a significant early milestone in his career. Passionate about both craft and mentorship, Rahul continues to inspire and guide aspiring designers through his practice and pedagogy.

A postgraduate from the National Institute of Design (NID), Paldi, and an undergraduate from the National Institute of Fashion Technology (NIFT), Bangalore, she has worked extensively across sectors including fashion, craft research, home furnishings, and education. Her areas of academic and creative expertise include surface ornamentation, functional fashion, traditional textile crafts, and game-based design learning.
entries.text: Rashmi Sridhara Assistant Professor Department of Design Rashmi Sridhara is a multidisciplinary Textile and Surface Designer, Print Maker, DIY Content Developer, and Educator with over a decade of professional experience in the design industry. A postgraduate from the National Institute of Design (NID), Paldi, and an undergraduate from the National Institute of Fashion Technology (NIFT), Bangalore, she has worked extensively across sectors including fashion, craft research, home furnishings, and education. Her areas of academic and creative expertise include surface ornamentation, functional fashion, traditional textile crafts, and game-based design learning.

entries.title: Ravi Nikam
entries.excerpt: Mr. Ravi Nikam is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Media & Communication at MIT-WPU, bringing extensive experience from both the media industry and academia. He believes that meaningful learning is achieved through a balance of theoretical understanding and hands-on practice. His teaching philosophy emphasizes experiential learning, critical thinking, ethical storytelling, and industry relevance, empowering students to become creative, socially responsible media professionals. Passionate about documentary filmmaking and visual storytelling, he is committed to fostering innovation, interdisciplinary collaboration, and research-oriented learning, contributing to MIT-WPU's vision of developing globally competent, socially conscious, and future-ready students.

entries.title: Renuka Amit Mane
entries.department: Department of Computer Engineering and Technology
entries.excerpt: Dr. Renuka Mane is an Assistant Professor at MIT World Peace University, Pune, with over 10 years of experience in Computer Engineering. She specializes in Brain-Computer Interface (BCI), IoT, Blockchain, and Network Security. She holds a PhD from Bharati Vidyapeeth (2023) and has published 20+ research papers in international journals and conferences. With multiple patents in BCI and network security, she actively mentors students and participates in faculty development programs. Her expertise includes machine learning, neural networks, and IoT frameworks, contributing significantly to research and innovation in emerging technologies.

entries.title: Research
entries.excerpt: Research themes, groups, and collaboration pathways in the Department of Biosciences and Technology at MIT-WPU.
entries.text: Research · Department of Biosciences and Technology Research Research themes, groups, and collaboration pathways in the Department of Biosciences and Technology at MIT-WPU. Biosciences & Technology Research The Department of Biosciences and Technology integrates molecular and cellular science, biotechnology, microbiology, bioinformatics, and translational research. 01 Cancer, cell & regenerative biology Cancer cell biology, genome stability, stem cells, tissue engineering, bone biology, and protein aggregation. 02 Genomics, bioinformatics & microbiomes Genomics, transcriptomics, metagenomics, microbial and human microbiomes, protein structure, and computational biology. 03 Microbiology, immunology & bioprocessing Applied and environmental microbiology, immune profiling, microbial metabolites, enzyme technology, and bioprocess development. 04 Plant molecular biology & sustainable biotechnology Plant development, defence and metabolism; plant–microbe interactions; genome editing; and crop improvement. 05 Biosensors, diagnostics & bioimaging Electrochemical and optical biosensors, pathogen and molecular diagnostics, bioimaging, and preclinical workflows.

entries.title: Research
entries.excerpt: Research themes, groups, and collaboration pathways in the Department of Chemistry at MIT-WPU.
entries.text: Research · Department of Chemistry Research Research themes, groups, and collaboration pathways in the Department of Chemistry at MIT-WPU. Chemistry Research The Department of Chemistry combines foundational chemical science and laboratory learning with faculty research in polymer science, synthesis and catalysis, functional and molecular materials, nanomaterials, environmental chemistry, and photonics. 01 Nanomaterials, molecular materials & photonics Nanomaterial synthesis, plasmonics and nanophotonics, metal-organic frameworks, molecular nanomagnets, switches, and fluorescent sensors. 02 Organic synthesis, catalysis & green chemistry Synthetic organic chemistry, ionic liquids, organocatalysis, heterogeneous catalysis, asymmetric synthesis, biocatalysis, and green synthesis. 03 Polymers, coatings & sustainable materials Liquid-crystalline and conducting polymers, biopolymers, nanocomposites, protective coatings, packaging, microplastics, and water purification. 04 Inorganic & environmental chemistry Metal complexes, wastewater treatment, pollutant degradation, and materials chemistry for environmental applications.

entries.title: Research
entries.excerpt: Research themes, groups, and collaboration pathways in the Department of Environmental Studies at MIT-WPU.
entries.text: Research · Department of Environmental Studies Research Research themes, groups, and collaboration pathways in the Department of Environmental Studies at MIT-WPU. Environmental Studies Research The department offers postgraduate and doctoral study spanning ecology and biodiversity, water and pollution, climate and sustainability, environmental assessment, and applied field research. 01 Ecology, biodiversity & conservation Urban ecology, biogeography, species and habitat conservation, biodiversity informatics, and community-led restoration. 02 Water, pollution & environmental engineering River- and lake-bank filtration, emerging contaminants, water and wastewater treatment, and sustainable water systems. 03 Climate action & sustainability assessment Sustainable development goals, environmental impact assessment, life-cycle assessment, ESG, natural-resource management, and environmental innovation. 04 Citizen science & environmental communication Public participation, biodiversity data, environmental education, and science communication for conservation action.

entries.title: Research
entries.excerpt: Research themes, groups, and collaboration pathways in the Department of Mathematics and Statistics at MIT-WPU.
entries.text: Research · Department of Mathematics and Statistics Research Research themes, groups, and collaboration pathways in the Department of Mathematics and Statistics at MIT-WPU. Mathematics & Statistics Research The Department of Mathematics and Statistics connects rigorous mathematical foundations with statistics, computation, optimisation, data science, and applications across science and engineering. 01 Discrete mathematics & combinatorics Matroid and graph theory, algebra, lattice theory, combinatorics, coding theory, and discrete structures. 02 Analysis, modelling & differential equations Geometric function theory, differential equations, numerical analysis, elastodynamics, wave propagation, fluid mechanics, and mathematical modelling. 03 Statistics, data science & machine learning Sampling, ranked-set sampling, statistical inference, Bayesian methods, statistical genetics, time series, biostatistics, data science, and machine learning. 04 Optimisation & operations research Optimisation, decision-making, transportation and supply-chain models, and fuzzy and rough sets.

entries.title: Research
entries.excerpt: Research themes, groups, and collaboration pathways in the Department of Physics at MIT-WPU.
entries.text: Research · Department of Physics Research Research themes, groups, and collaboration pathways in the Department of Physics at MIT-WPU. Physics Research The Department of Physics brings together teaching, laboratory learning, and research across fundamental, computational, optical, materials, and device physics. 01 Quantum, optics & photonics Nonlinear and quantum optics, structured light, nanophotonics, photonic systems, plasmonics, and spectroscopy. Photonics Research Group Quantum Science & Technology Group 02 Materials, devices & nanoscience Semiconductor and dielectric thin films, nanomaterials and nanocomposites, sensors, photodetectors, and functional devices. 03 Astrophysics & fundamental physics Cosmic magnetic fields, galactic dynamics, high-energy theory, gravitation, and the mathematical structure of fundamental interactions. 04 Applied & computational physics Soft matter, quantum chemistry and computational physics; radiation processing of polymers; liquid acoustics; dielectric spectroscopy; and corrosion science.

entries.title: Rucha Deshpande
entries.excerpt: Generative AI, User Experience, Design Education
entries.text: Rucha Deshpande Assistant Professor Department of Design Generative AI, User Experience, Design Education Rucha Deshpande is a seasoned design educator with over 14 years of experience in design strategy, user experience, and creative thinking. She has worked with leading brands such as Lifestyles and Reliance Trends as a Concept Designer for retail store experiences before transitioning into academia with institutions including the Symbiosis Institute of Design and MIT-WPU, Pune. An alumna of NID Ahmedabad and a certified UX Designer from IIT Delhi, she brings a strong foundation in user-centred design and cross-functional collaboration.

entries.text: Sachin Ratnakar Deshmukh Assistant Professor Department of Mechanical Engineering Additive Manufacturing, Hybrid Manufacturing Meet Sachin Ratnakar Deshmukh, an Assistant Professor in Mechanical Engineering (Workshop) with more than 15 years of experience, including 5 years in reputed industries. Sachin is currently pursuing his Ph.D. and holds an MTech degree. His expertise lies in the fields of Additive Manufacturing and Hybrid Manufacturing. entries.title: Sagar Sunil Kolapkar
entries.excerpt: Transportation materials, Warm Mix Asphalt, Parking Policy and Demand Studies
entries.text: Sagar Sunil Kolapkar Assistant Professor Department of Civil Engineering Transportation materials, Warm Mix Asphalt, Parking Policy and Demand Studies Prof. Sagar Kolapkar is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Civil Engineering, MIT World Peace University, Pune. He received his B.E. in Civil Engineering (2010) from Pune University and M.Tech (Transportation Systems Engineering) in 2013 from IIT Guwahati. entries.text: Vanita Raj Tank Assistant Professor Department of Electrical and Electronics Engineering Signal Processing, Embedded Systems, Digital Communication Vanita Raj Tank is an Assistant Professor at the School of Electronics and Communication. She holds an M.Tech degree in Digital Communication from MANIT Bhopal. Her research work includes a project on "Noise Reduction for Ultrasound wave" conducted at CWPRS Khadakwasla, Pune. She has published research papers in various international conferences and two publications in Quartile journals. Her areas of expertise include Signal Processing, Embedded Systems, and Digital Communication. 1.Adaptive recurrent nonnegative matrix factorization with phase compensation for Single- Channel speech enhancement, 2022 VR Tank, SP Mahajan, Multimedia Tools and Applications 81 (20), 28249-28294 2.Under water noise reduction using wavelet and savitzky-golay, 2014 S Balan, A Khaparde, V Tank, T Rade, K Takalkar Second international conference on computational science and engineering 243250 3. Speech enhancement using auditory-based transform, 2015 VR Tank, A Khaparde, SP Mahajan, R Deshpande 10th International Conference on Information, Communications and Signal 4. Implementation of Auditory Filter Bank for Denoising Speech Signal using Simulink and MATLAB, 2017, P Saboo, VR Tank, SP Mahajan,International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control

entries.title: Vijaya Vijaykumar Pangave
entries.excerpt: Embedded Systems
entries.text: Vijaya Vijaykumar Pangave Lecturer (Selection Grade) Department of Polytechnic Embedded Systems Mrs. Vijaya Pangave is a highly experienced and knowledgeable Senior Lecturer in the E/Tc department, with a total teaching experience of 25.8 years. She holds an M.Tech in Communication System and a B.E. in E&TC. Mrs. Pangave specializes in subjects such as MIC, EEC, ECN, BEM, and LIC, and currently works as an Assistant Professor in MITWPU, School of Polytechnic & Skill Development. With her expertise in the field of electronics and communication, she has proven to be an asset to her students and colleagues alike. 1. IoT Based Smart Saffron Cultivation System 2. Advanced Telemetry System for Hospital Wagon with Fog Cloud Operability 3. Cloud based IIoT Solution for Shop Floor Management System 4. Arduino Controlled Surveillance Robot

entries.title: Vishakha Vivek Pethkar
entries.excerpt: Ms. Vishakha Pethkar holds a Masters Degree in English Literature and is currently pursuing her PhD in Queer studies. With a passion for teaching, she specializes in literature, communication and literary criticism. Her unique perspective and innovative teaching methods have made her a popular figure among her students. She has presented her research papers in various national and international seminars and conferences, and has contributed to multiple publications. With her expertise in the field, she is constantly striving to bring new insights and knowledge to her students.

entries.title: Vrunda Hemant Agarkar
entries.excerpt: Prof. Vrunda Agarkar is working as an Assistant Professor at the Department of Civil Engineering at Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University, Pune. She is presently pursuing her PhD with SVNIT, Surat. She received her BE (Civil) degree in the year 1997 and M E (Civil) degree in the year 1999 from SVNIT, Surat. She joined MIT-WPU in the year 2007. Since then, she has taught various subjects like Basic Civil and Environmental Engineering, Applied Mechanics, Fluid Mechanics, Momentum Transfer, Surveying, Fluid Mechanics 2, Hydrology and Water Resource Engineering, Strength of Material, Civil Engineering and Survey Methods, Introduction to Civil Engineering (Elective F.Y.B. Tech).
